Each protagonist gets 10 points to spend to give themselves limitations. The limitations are listed on page 80 and onward.

Step 10: Traits
All protagonists have traits—characteristics that show who they are. These traits can be both negative and positive. Everything from physical appearance, personality, events during upbringing, genetic advantages or tragic handicaps have shaped the person into who he or she is upon entry into the story.

Each protagonist gets 10 points to use to give themselves traits. The traits are listed on page 84 and onward.

Step 11: Character Traits
Character traits show a person's personality and behavior. They are in themselves neither positive nor negative, but can influence the person's reasoning and actions in different situations—for example, a greedy person has difficulty resisting a bribe.

It is appropriate for a person to have between one and three character traits, although more can be chosen if it fits the concept. See page 225.

### The Character Traits

• Aggressive • Arrogant • Cautious
• Greedy • Honorable • Vengeful
• Loving • Indifferent • Lustful
• Suspicious • Treacherous • Risk-prone
• Convinced

### Limitations and traits

**It is important that players and narrator together analyze and think through the choices made, so that it both fits into the story as a whole and into the person's life. Most of the choices are general and can be interpreted in several different ways to fit. It can be interesting to know when in a person's life things happen or have happened—having poor vision is probably a genetic condition the person was born with, while having a family can affect the person in very different ways depending on whether he acquired it recently or if it happened long ago in his/her life.**

It may very well be that a player has a concept that requires more choices than he/she has points for. Then the player and narrator should discuss this and see if it's okay to change the sum of points so the concept fits. The important thing is to ensure that the person doesn't become completely unplayable or parodic. In the end, it's still about playing with a protagonist you enjoy and who fits into the story to be told.

### Greater character trait impact

**The character traits can be made more pronounced and then take an even larger place in the person's life and personality. By giving a character trait the prefix very or extremely, one amplifies its degree of impact. For example, a person instead of being honorable and aggressive can become very honorable and extremely aggressive.**

A person should not have more than two character traits with the prefix "very" or more than one with the prefix "extremely", unless exceptionally good reasons exist.

### Bear in mind that character traits that are extreme have very great impact on how the person acts, so they should be used sparingly and with consideration. People with several extreme character traits tend to be difficult to play and exaggerated, and this can easily ruin the story.

**The narrator must approve all character traits that have prefixes.**

### Expression

When a trait is chosen, its expression must also be determined. The expression a trait takes provides increased depth and an extra dimension to the character and makes it easy to immerse yourself in him/her as it shows how and/or in which situations the trait manifests. All chosen traits shall have at least one expression. A trait can have several expressions, but usually the number is between one and three. For examples and information regarding expressions, see page 228.

Step 12: Relationships
A protagonist knows three narrator characters from the start. The player should work together with the narrator to create these characters so they fit into upcoming stories and into the protagonist's concept. The narrator gets to assess the reasonability of the proposed narrator characters.

A narrator character may not have any blood ties to the protagonist or be his/her spouse, as this is reserved for the limitation family (see page 81).

The three narrator characters are noted in the protagonist's character sheet. Their respective name, profession, and concept are determined freely while Influence and Relationship are set to "Minor" and "Hostile" respectively. The player then gets 10 level points. Each level point can improve either Influence or Relationship by one level, see tables 1:4 and 1:5. More about narrator characters, relationships, and influence can be found on page 204.

### Suggestions for suitable relationships

• Ex-partner
• Former employer
• Former business partner
• Friend/acquaintance
• Childhood friend
• Lover
• Assistant/henchman
• Rival
• Mentor
• Professional contact

### Level Points Value Corresponds to

0 6 Minor influence
1 9 Moderate influence
2 12 Influential
3 15 Significant influence
4 18 Very great influence
Table 1:4

### Level Points Relationship

0 Hostile
1 Disapproving
2 Neutral
3 Favorable
4 Friendly
Table 1:5

Step 13: Attributes & attribute values
Attributes are a kind of secondary information about the character that is used during gameplay. Some of the attributes are used for rolls, while others are used as references for other circumstances - also for calculating other attribute values. For more detailed information about attributes, see page 218.

### Attribute values

Each attribute has an attribute value that is determined in different ways, usually by adding together the skill in different abilities, according to table 1:6. All attribute values are rounded down to the nearest whole number. A recommendation is to determine Size first as it is used in other formulas.

Every time a skill that is part of a formula changes, the attribute values governed by the formula must also be recalculated.

If an attribute value is affected by any additional factor, for example a trait or an area of expertise, this change is always applied after the possible formula, or base value, has been determined.

### Attribute Attribute Value

Defense (Intuition + Reactions + Awareness)/3
Learning (Determination + Reasoning + Worldliness)/3
Impression (Charm or Leadership + Sense + Worldliness)/3
Standard of Living Usually 4, see page 86
Life Will (Determination + Constitution + Sense)/3 + 5
Notoriety Usually Unknown (5), see page 86
Damage Bonus (Size + Strength)/10
Size Usually Medium (15), see page 87
Combat Movement (Determination + Constitution + Mobility)/3
Wound Threshold (Constitution + Size + Strength)/3
Luck Usually 0, see page 87
Endurance (Resistance + Size + Strength)/3 + 5
Appearance Usually Ordinary (±0), see page 87
Well-being Usually 10 out of maximum 10, see page 80
Fate Usually 10, see page 87
Overview (Reactions + Sense + Awareness)/3
Table 1:6

Step 14: Deepening
The deepening is for further exploring the protagonist's emotional life, upbringing, experiences, and personality. It can take quite a bit of time and is not necessary for character creation - but it gives the protagonist a multifaceted background and a complex depth.

Step 15: Name
Determine what the protagonist shall be called. The name says a lot about the person in question and if it becomes the "wrong" name, this can disturb the experience. Avoid inappropriate, silly, famous, or historical names.

Step 16: Money & equipment
The protagonist receives a sum of cash based on his/her standard of living, usually 5,000 zovriner. The character also gets a starting set of items that illustrates that he/she has lived a life before the story starts. He/she can also acquire additional possessions. More about possessions can be found on page 88 and onward.

### Variants regarding relationships

**A narrator can choose that each** protagonist has more or fewer than three relationships from the start. The narrator can also assign narrator characters to a protagonist, for example if the narrator wants to tie in a narrator character he/she knows will appear in a future story and thus get a stronger connection to the protagonist.

Attraction & Passion
A player who wants his/her protagonist to have an emotionally turbulent relationship from the start can give the relationship an attraction and/or a passion, according to page 208. This costs one level point for an attraction and an additional level point for a passion.

### Successor

**An interesting variant that can** be used regarding relationships is that the player determines a successor to his/her protagonist. This means that one of the relationships is set in advance as a possible successor (that is, a new protagonist) if the current protagonist should disappear from a story, for example via death, kidnapping, retirement, flight, madness, or serious injury.

**The advantage of this is that the** classic stylistic elements revenge, searching, and mystery get a natural entry into the story. Often it can be difficult to introduce new protagonists into an ongoing story in a non-forced way.

**If a successor is already designated** among the protagonist's relationships, the connection becomes credible and falls naturally into the story.

**A narrator can also let all** protagonists get an extra relationship from the start, provided that it is precisely the successor. Otherwise, the successor has no special rules but functions exactly like an ordinary relationship to the protagonist.

### Name suggestions

On page 158 there is information about naming cultures in Imperiet.

The deepening is for further exploring the protagonist's emotional life, upbringing, experiences, and personality. It can take quite a bit of time and is not necessary for character creation - but it gives the protagonist a multifaceted background and a complex depth.

History & psychological profile
To give even more flesh on the bones, here follows a number of questions that the character reasonably should be able to answer. There are no right or wrong answers to the questions, but they are meant to help the player and narrator think in the right direction and to give the character the depth he/she deserves.

What does the protagonist's family, relatives & similar relationships look like?
Does the protagonist have a large family or is he/she perhaps alone in the world? How does the family interact? Which holidays, festivities, and gatherings are celebrated? Do family members help each other or is there bad blood between different parts of the family? Are there other close ones who don't share blood ties with the protagonist but who still figure in his/her vicinity - like perhaps the shop owner on the corner or "uncle" Tatarin?

What were the protagonist's upbringing conditions like?
Where was the young protagonist born and raised? Was it a protected, safe, and secure life or was it unsafe, with violence, shame, and anxiety? Was there fighting in his/her vicinity, were there bitter quarrels, divorces, disputes, alcoholism, abuse, violence, or assaults involved? How were the economic conditions - even in well-off homes emotional coldness and contempt are born?

What are the most important/most significant events in the protagonist's life?
It is often individual events that make the protagonist choose certain paths in life - identifying some such events is of great importance when the character is developed. What has happened in the protagonist's life? Are there any events that played a major role during upbringing? **Perhaps a childhood friend's death or the adoption** of a pet? Or is there some other occurrence that has made a great impact - perhaps the first kiss, a brutal assault, the first car theft, or killing in self-defense?

Perhaps a role model died suddenly, perhaps the protagonist was betrayed by a comrade, or perhaps a love turned out to be an unfaithful bastard?

What does the protagonist value most highly?
Is there something that is the absolutely most important thing in the protagonist's life? It can be anything from a photo of a lost love to spending time together with near and dear ones. Perhaps it is material status that is held highest, or a career with title, authority, and power. An ideal and a motto - to always protect the weak or to never strike women? To never fall into the detestable patterns one has experienced during one's upbringing and pass these on to one's own children? An object - a vintage car, a family heirloom, or maintaining the family's good name and reputation? The more valuable something is, the more the protagonist will fight for it.

What traits/characteristics does the protagonist dislike in others?
All people like something about others. What is often called personal chemistry governs whom the protagonist prefers to associate with. Some people simply cannot get along. Mention some peculiarities, personality traits, or behaviors that the protagonist simply has very difficulty handling, accepting, or going along with. It can be a dissolute lifestyle, contemptuous attitudes and prejudices, boastful loudmouths, flirtatious slime, ingratiating toadies, lazy coworkers, or perhaps larger groups like women, men, bosses, or anything - but something is always there that disturbs the protagonist to the extreme.

### What dark secrets or guilty conscience does the protagonist have?

Does the protagonist have a bad conscience about something he/she did in the past? Or perhaps about something he/she didn't do? An anxiety that sometimes scratches superficially and sometimes tears paralyzing wounds in the soul? A dark secret just waiting to leak out? Has the character hurt someone, betrayed a friend for fleeting sex or dirty money, missed an important family dinner, looked the other way when a terrible crime was committed, or perhaps embezzled money from the poor and needy?

Perhaps the protagonist didn't take the chance to ask out the one he/she had yearned for for several years before it was too late? Perhaps the protagonist worked overtime one evening instead of going home to the family, and therefore couldn't protect them from the murderer who extinguished their lives? Or is the protagonist tormented by the memory of that evening, after the wild party, when he/she ran over a homeless girl and fled the scene? Or is it perhaps something shameful that hides beneath the surface - like that a brother and sister have a secret love affair or that the character is in debt and caught in the hands of an unscrupulous loan shark?

Personality & traits
The player and narrator should figure out some characterizing traits that fit the protagonist's personality. Most people prefer certain things while they dislike others, or they dress or behave in a certain way.

Here follows a number of suggestions for different distinguishing traits a character can possess. These should be seen as help for bringing out the character in the story and for providing support in creating an image of the character, both for the player, the narrator, and fellow players. There are no rules governing these personality traits and a character can have none, one, or several, depending on what the image of the character is.

Appearance/clothing
The character has a characteristic appearance or a preference for a special type of clothing. Overall, the character's appearance and presentation are important for being able to create an image of him/her. Here follows a number of suggestions for distinctive appearance and/or clothing, but it is of course free to come up with your own.

• Always wears blood-red lipstick
• Always wears expensive clothes
• Always wears a skirt
• Always wears sexually provocative clothes
• Always wears a white shirt
• Always wears gloves
• Always wears a scarf
• Always wears a hat
• Prefers clothes in a certain color
• Always has a carnation in the lapel
• Always wears expensive jewelry
• Always has stubble
• Always has visible wounds for some reason
• Has bad/perfect complexion
• Has a mustache or beard (or both)
• Has no hair at all (anywhere?)
• Has a clean-shaven face
• Has wild and uncombed hair
• Has a special hairstyle (slicked, curly, permed, ponytail, braids, etc.)
• Never leaves his/her worn long coat
• Never seen without a pair of sunglasses
• Refuses to wear shoes other than stiletto heels
• Is always meticulously made up
• Is a fashion icon - only the latest may be worn
• Is visibly scarred

### Drives

**Drives are the personal goals** a character has. Usually the character develops drives during the course of the story in response to the events that occur. But if the character has a well-developed and thought-through background, it can very well be appropriate to give the character one or more drives from the start. See page 230 for more information about drives.

### Behaviors

The person carries one or more distinctive behaviors and mannerisms. Here follows a number of suggestions for prominent behaviors, but you are of course free to come up with your own.
• Easily becomes obsessed (with sex, a person, an object, a goal, et cetera)
• Single-minded in thought—once you've decided, you've decided
• Cries when tension releases, for example after a confrontation
• Always rocks on their chair
• Goes nowhere without being armed
• Has a cigar in the corner of their mouth, usually unlit
• Chain-smokes constantly
• Easily feels guilt (as soon as they might be the cause of something bad)
• Sensitive about something, for example height, weight, size of nose, hair color, et cetera
• Always puts their feet up on the table
• Principled—even if it causes problems
• Always flips a coin at every opportunity
• Sits with their back to the wall when out at a bar
• Throws around special recurring phrases, like "By Jestan!" or "That's hot!"
• Curses in every sentence
• Almost always angry/happy/gloomy/grumpy/et cetera
• Constantly seeks confirmation from others for self-esteem
• Comfort eats
• Constantly chews on a toothpick
• Chews gum
• Creature of habit (must read the morning paper at breakfast, and/or always goes to the same barber at the same regular time)
• Protective of something, for example children/women/
the vulnerable/homeless/elderly/et cetera
• Eats the same food day in and day out
• Only eats specific food
**Irritating quirks**
The person possesses one or more quirks that their surroundings may find disturbing and irritating. Here follows a number of suggestions for such traits, but you are of course free to come up with your own.
• Bites their nails
• Always flirts, even when it absolutely doesn't suit the situation, or when there's a rush.
• Makes pessimistic comments about everything and everyone, is never satisfied or positive
• Has dirty nails, regardless of whether they wash their hands
• Likes to crack their knuckles
• Cracks their jaw
• Cracks their neck now and then
• Picks their nose
• Burps and passes gas
• Calculates odds on all activities and actions.
• Must always compete as soon as there's a chance—first to finish eating, first to the door, et cetera
Despises/admires
The person carries prejudices and despises phenomena or people. In the same way, the person can harbor great admiration 
and respect for phenomena and individuals. Here follows a number of suggestions for people, groups, organizations or phenomena a person can admire or despise. You are of course free to come up with your own.
• Specific person/group/organization
• The unemployed
• Children in general
• Criminals/gangsters
• Animals
• Drugs
• Refugees/foreigners
• Women in general
• The Church/priests
• The law
• Men in general
• Money
• The police/police officers
• Prostitutes
• Racists
• Sex
• Hospitals/doctors
• The state/authorities/bureaucrats
• Beggars
• Entertainers
• Charity
Likes/dislikes
An individual always has something they like or dislike. It can be anything under the sun. Often the person has no problem interacting with things or people they don't like—it's just a general dislike and no deep fear. Here follows a number of suggestions for things one can like or dislike, but you are of course free to come up with your own.
• Alcohol
• Going to bars and meeting people
• Reading books
• Banks
• Children
• Cars
• Dogs
• Journalists
• Cats
• Knives
• Artworks
• The war
• Opera
• Rats
• Sex
• Hospitals
• Firearms
• Makeup
• Gambling and betting
• Sports
• Water

Creating a protagonist
To clarify the creation process, here follows a detailed example that takes into account both game mechanics and rules as well as the player's thoughts about creating a personality and emotional life for their protagonist.
**New protagonist**
Marco is going to play Noir and his narrator Petter has informed him that the intended stories lie close to classic film noir, with certain supernatural elements.
After talking for a while, the two arrive at the conclusion that Marco's protagonist should be a shady figure, with one foot on the side of the law and the other in the underworld. Some form of consultant who takes jobs but isn't under anyone's yoke.
Petter says that all protagonists at the start of the chronicle will begin with Life experience 0, see page 220.
Step 1: Preparations (p. 27)
Petter gives Marco an empty character sheet while Marco takes out a pencil and an eraser.
Step 2 & 3: Archetype/background package (p. 27)
In Noir, you're given the option to choose an archetype that becomes a framework for creation. Marco, however, wants to choose completely freely without the help that archetypes provide, which is perfectly fine. Thus Marco also skips step 3, the background packages.
Step 4: Concept (p. 27)
The protagonist needs to be summarized through a concept. After some pondering, Marco gives his protagonist 
the concept "Sharp consultant/detective". The protagonist 
is considered well-versed in the world he lives in
(that the protagonist is male has emerged during the initial conversations), hence the sharp nature. The choice of consultant/detective as occupation provides dynamism in what kind of person the protagonist is—one who takes jobs for payment in various fields.
Step 5: Attributes & attribute values (p. 28)
Now the protagonist's basic data should be set up through definition of attributes and attribute values.
The nine values available to distribute are 6, 5, 4, 4, 4, 3,
3, 3 and 2. Marco sees the protagonist as a competent shooter, a guy who can handle himself socially and who isn't completely clueless. No particular weakness presents itself spontaneously.

The two highest values, 6 and 5, are placed on Motorics and Charisma respectively, to create prerequisites for a shooter who can talk to people. The three 4s are set on Build, Perception and Insight. The motivation behind this is that Marco sees the protagonist as well-trained 
and in good shape. Additionally, the protagonist is attentive to what happens around him and has a developed gut feeling. Insight provides the ability to resist social influence and have a grip on the situation so it feels right to put a relatively high value on that attribute.
Three 3s and one 2 remain now. A 3 is put on Coordination 
so that the protagonist can manage if smooth movements or brawls are required. The next 3 is put on Creativity 
to give him imagination and resourcefulness. The last
3 is placed, after much deliberation, on Education, as Marco sees the protagonist as a reasonably well-read person, with schooling and general education in his background. This means that the 2 is placed on Will. At first it doesn't feel so good, but after some pondering and discussions with Petter, Marco arrives at the conclusion that it becomes a good hook for the protagonist, 
a twist in the personality. A doubting soul behind a harder exterior.
Marco also takes the opportunity to update the protagonist's 
concept to "Sharp but doubting consultant/detective".
Step 6: Skills & skill values (p. 28)
By doubling an attribute's attribute value you get how many points you receive to increase the attribute's associated skills. In Marco's case it looks as follows:
Coordination (3); gives 6 points.
Build (4); gives 8 points.
Motorics (6); gives 12 points.
Education (3); gives 6 points.
Creativity (3); gives 6 points.
Will (2); gives 4 points.
Insight (4); gives 8 points.
Perception (4); gives 8 points.
Charisma (5); gives 10 points.
Marco looks at table 1:2 on page 28 and notes that a skill value of 4–6 corresponds to "Competent" and a skill 
value of 7–9 corresponds to "Professional". This gives an indication of what the levels represent.
He begins with the attribute Motorics, where he has 12
points. The three skills under Motorics are Manual dexterity, 
Throwing weapons and Firearms. For seven points the skill 
value in Firearms is raised to 7. Five points remain.
Four of these go to raising Manual dexterity to 4 and the last point raises Throwing weapons to 1.
After Marco has gone through the rest of the attributes and skills, the point distribution looks as follows:

**Coordination (3):** Melee (4), Reactions (1), Mobility (1)

**Build (4):** Endurance (4), Strength (4), Tolerance (0)

**Motorics (6):** Manual dexterity (4), Throwing weapons (1), Firearms (7)

**Education (3):** Humanities (2), Medicine (2), Natural science (2)

**Creativity (3):** Construction (1), Create (1), Deduction (4)

**Will (2):** Determination (0), Resistance (1), Sense (3)

**Insight (4):** Empathy (1), Oppose (3), Streetwise (4)

**Perception (4):** Intuition (4), Vehicles (0), Awareness (4)

**Charisma (5):** Charm (1), Leadership (4), Persuade (5)
That the skill values under Education are distributed evenly indicates a broad general basic knowledge.
That Tolerance has 0 in skill value means that the protagonist's 
body isn't particularly resistant to diseases, poisons or drugs—probably the protagonist 
has been quite spared from such things since the value isn't higher.
Since Persuade and Leadership are prioritized over Charm, the protagonist is quite direct in his approach and doesn't engage much in diplomatic maneuvering. A thought was born when the skill value in Leadership was determined, namely that the protagonist has previously been in the army, for example as an officer, and that it was there that physique, shooting and leadership 
ability were formed. But due to low skill values in
Resistance and Determination he didn't cope very well with this career, 
he had trouble giving orders in pressing 
situations and couldn't bear the horrors of war.
Step 7: Proficiencies (p. 28)
Proficiencies are obtained by summing the respective skill value with the governing attribute's attribute value.
The protagonist's proficiencies in Marco's case are shown in table 1:7.
**Attribute value**
Skill Proficiency + Skill value

| Attribute/Skill | Attribute Value | Proficiency |
|---|---|---|
| Determination | 2 | 2+0 |
| Charm | 6 | 5+1 |
| Empathy | 5 | 4+1 |
| Manual dexterity | 10 | 6+4 |
| Vehicles | 4 | 4+0 |
| Humanities | 5 | 3+2 |
| Intuition | 8 | 4+4 |
| Throwing weapons | 7 | 6+1 |
| Endurance | 8 | 4+4 |
| Construction | 4 | 3+1 |
| Leadership | 9 | 5+4 |
| Medicine | 5 | 3+2 |
**The table continues on the next page**

| Attribute/Skill | Attribute Value | Proficiency |
|---|---|---|
| Oppose | 7 | 4+3 |
| Natural science | 5 | 3+2 |
| Melee | 7 | 3+4 |
| Reactions | 4 | 3+1 |
| Resistance | 3 | 2+1 |
| Mobility | 4 | 3+1 |
| Sense | 5 | 2+3 |
| Create | 4 | 3+1 |
| Firearms | 13 | 6+7 |
| Deduction | 7 | 3+4 |
| Strength | 8 | 4+4 |
| Tolerance | 4 | 4+0 |
| Awareness | 8 | 4+4 |
| Streetwise | 8 | 4+4 |
| Persuade | 10 | 5+5 |
Table 1:7
Step 8: Expertises (p. 28)
Expertise is specialized competence or specialization within a skill. All skills that have a skill value between 
4–6 get one expertise, and those with skill value 7+ get two expertises. In Marco's case this means that the protagonist gets expertises in the skills indicated in table 1:8. The table also shows the chosen expertises.

| Skill | Skill Value | Expertises |
|---|---|---|
| Manual dexterity | 4 | 1—Lock picking: Door locks (p. 63) |
| Intuition | 4 | 1—Instinct (p. 64) |
| Endurance | 4 | 1—Good shape (p. 66) |
| Leadership | 4 | 1—Rhetoric: The army (p. 68) |
| Melee | 4 | 1—Amateur boxing (p. 69) |
| Firearms | 7 | 2—Battle-tested (p. 75), Weapon handling: Pistol (p. 75) |
| Deduction | 4 | 1—Clear-minded: Tactics/strategy (p. 75) |
| Strength | 4 | 1—Well-trained (p. 77) |
| Awareness | 4 | 1—Observant: Notice discrepancies/details (p. 78) |
| Streetwise | 4 | 1—Knows the score (p. 78) |
| Persuade | 5 | 1—Persuasive: Convince/plead (p. 79) |
Table 1:8
The expertises were chosen based on the image of the protagonist; a former officer in the army (Rhetoric: The army, Weapon handling: 
Pistol, Battle-tested, Clear-minded: Tactics/strategy), a guy who keeps his body in shape (Well-trained, Good shape, Amateur 
boxing) and who can handle himself socially and be aware of things (Persuasive: Convince/plead, Knows the score). Also the thoughts about the protagonist being involved in the action and having gut feeling are present (Observant: 
Notice discrepancies/details, Instinct). An expertise that hasn't been touched upon earlier during creation is
Lock picking, something that came as a new experience for the protagonist in this step.
The expertises help to shape the protagonist and show in a much clearer way than attribute values, skill 
values and proficiencies where his competencies lie.
Step 9: Limitations (p. 28)
Marco receives ten points that must be spent on limitations. 
After some reading he chooses the following:
• Noticeable weakness: Women (Beautiful women): 2 points.
The protagonist likes women, is drawn to them and spellbound by them, especially beautiful women. This is a sure source of trouble.
• Tangible fear: Thick fog/smoke/gas: 3 points
• Nightmares: 2 points
The fear and nightmares stem from the war, Marco decides that when the protagonist finds himself in a situation 
where visibility is severely limited by fog, smoke or gas clouds he risks the fear taking over—a side effect from the countless gas attacks at the front. The nightmares the protagonist has are about the horrors he has seen and experienced on the battlefields.
• Indebted (Completely): 2 points
Indebtedness means that the protagonist is in debt to a person, a company or an organization. The debt doesn't need to be further defined at this stage. However, it is a good hook to build personal stories on.
• Basic code of honor
Not harm women or children: 1 point
The protagonist has a basic moral compass, a personal 
goal to never harm women or children. With this the ten points have been used.
Step 10: Traits (p. 28)
Also for traits Marco receives ten points that must be spent. 
On suggestion from the narrator Marco focuses on determining Size, Appearance and Standard of living first.
• Tall (Size 21), height approximately 190 cm, weight approximately 90 kg: 2 points
• Standard of living 6: 2 points
• Attractive appearance: 1 point
The protagonist is tall (Size 21). The height and weight are freely determined based on concept and background. The protagonist 
lives relatively well (Standard of living 6)
with his own apartment and decent belongings. He looks relatively 
good (Attractive) which can provide advantages in social situations where appearance matters. Five points remain.
• Flaw 1 extra point
Among Flaws & Handicaps Marco sees that one can get more points. "Maximum five extra" Petter informs him. Marco chooses that the protagonist has a Flaw. The flaw concerns the skill
Sense and comes from the protagonist's traumatic background. 
This gives an extra point, total six points.
• Charming: 2 points
• Hardy: 2 points
For four points the protagonist gets two Talents & Gifts. 
Charming improves the first impression he makes on others, Hardy shows a physical ability to endure beatings and blows better than most. Two points remain.

"That night she used my body as a bandage."
—Gabriel Jandaran

• Reputation: Noted (6): Underworld: 2 points
Marco wants the protagonist to be known in the underworld as a reliable and dependable guy, a man you can trust when it counts. This consumes the last points. More points can be acquired through certain other features that give bonus points, but Marco thinks it feels good and sensible as it is.

Step 11: Character Traits (p. 28)
Marco reads through the list of character traits. Three of them, with their respective expressions (p. 224), feel right.
• Arrogant (Vain)
• Very Greedy (Hungers for money)
• Loving (Understanding, Accommodating)

The arrogance comes from a measure of vanity. Marco decides that the protagonist is a bit of a clothes snob and takes care of his appearance—always clean-shaven, clean nails, properly dressed in quality clothes from respectable brands, polished shoes, etc. "If you radiate class, you get classy jobs," as the saying goes.

The lust for money is a dominant character trait and therefore gets the prefix "Very." Money governs many of the protagonist's choices in life and he has difficulty saying no when money is offered for services or jobs.

Loving is perhaps a slightly unexpected character trait but it feels right—the protagonist gives a professional, interested impression, he has a smooth manner and an understanding of the client's needs and wishes.

Step 12: Relationships (p. 29)
Three relationships shall be established, with name, trade, and concept. If the protagonist had had a family from Step 9: Limitations, members from this would have been added in this step. Each shall have a Relationship to the protagonist and a measure of their ability to perform actions, called Influence. Ten level points Marco shall use to increase these two values among the relationships.

Domenik "Manik" Jaeger, boastful but reliable smuggler/dealer. Relationship: Favorable (3 level points), Influence: 12 (2 level points)
Manik is the protagonist's fence, a person who deals in most things and who has contacts on the street. The relationship has developed and is relatively personal—Manik sometimes gives the protagonist information or tips for free, something very rare in Sandukar. The two help each other when things get critical.

Doctor Yannik Reasescu, former client, private physician with his own clinic, has access to drugs. Relationship: Neutral (2 level points), Influence: 9 (1 level point)
Doctor Reasescu was one of the protagonist's early cases, a client in a matter concerning finding a missing person. The case proceeded positively and the good doctor considers the protagonist to be a solid person, so he'll help out if medical care is needed outside the normal channels.

Talia Navarre, scorned and wounded ex-fiancée, manipulative and driven. Relationship: Hostile (0 level points), Influence: 9 (1 level point), Attraction (1 level point)
Talia is the woman the protagonist abandoned at the altar. She is from a fine family with connections within House Guiskard and she didn't take the incident particularly well. She and the protagonist still have heated feelings for each other. But since wounded feelings are always close to the surface, the result is often fighting and quarreling.

Step 13: Attributes & Attribute Values (p. 29)
Attributes and attribute values provide various information about the protagonist. Most are based on the protagonist's Skills. The formulas for calculation are given on page 29. In Marco's case it looks according to table 1:9.

### Attribute Attribute Value

Defense 4 (sum 6, −2 from Sturdy Size)
Learning 5
Impression 9 (sum 7, +2 from Charming)
Standard of Living 6 (From Feature: Standard of Living)
Life Will 10
Reputation Noted (6) (From Feature: Reputation)
Damage Increase 3
Size Sturdy (22) (21 from Feature: Size, +1 from Well-Trained)
Combat Movement 4
Wound Threshold 12
Luck 0
Endurance 20 (sum 16, +1 from Good Shape, +1 from Amateur Boxing, +2 from Tough)
Appearance Attractive (±1) (From Feature: Appearance)
Well-Being Relaxed (10)
Fate 10
Overview 6 (sum 5, +1 from Combat Experienced)

Table 1:9

The attribute value for Defense is also noted at Ranged and Melee respectively—these are the values used during combat. The expertise in Amateur Boxing gives +1 to Defense in Melee so the numbers become: Defense Ranged: 4 and Defense Melee: 5.

Step 14: Deepening (p. 29)
Parts of what is included in the Deepening step have already been addressed in other parts of the creation process but are also covered here so nothing is missed.

In this step, motivations are chosen among other things. Marco wants his protagonist to have an established motivation (p. 230) from the start.
• Achieve goal: Become debt-free (Burning)

It fits well into the background and provides reasonable hooks for dramatic stories.

Regarding the protagonist's psychological profile, several of the questions are summarized in one long answer. The protagonist grew up in a middle-class home in the capital. His mother was a housewife and his father worked as a foreman at a factory in the city. The protagonist's only sibling, a younger sister, ran away nearly 15 years ago and has been missing since.

The upbringing was fairly secure and the protagonist did acceptably in school. After a few years of wavering in higher education he dropped out, to his father's dismay and his mother's horror, as he wanted to do his patriotic duty and enlist, eagerly encouraged by the Ministry of Truth's posters and information films at the cinemas. During basic training a major took him under his wing and gave him the chance to train directly as an officer, which he completed.

As a newly minted lieutenant he traveled to the northern front for a couple years of hell—comrades were killed or maimed, insane orders and equally insane attacks were carried out, and during the years he led thousands of young boys into the jaws of death, not many meters of land were conquered.

After a successful storming of an enemy bunker the protagonist was promoted to captain. But during the assault several of his closest friends died and psychosis became a reality. He increasingly sought solace and relief in liquor and whores, something that only added to the anguish. After a complete breakdown during an offensive in pouring rain and thick mud, the protagonist was furloughed home and ended up at a sanatorium in Sandukar. The protagonist doesn't know who arranged this, but suspects the old major from before.

It took him over a year to become human again. He rejected the idea of returning to the front and instead moved into the city's soul in search of housing and livelihood. He hustled jobs through postings at pubs and beer cafés, sold himself cheap to build up a reputation as a stable and reliable resource. Some jobs involved danger, others were simple, some led him into darkness, others to light and warmth.

Through the years his contact network was built up, more and more money and prestige came the protagonist's way, but also memories from before. Some jobs he never took, like going after women or children. Parts of the old destructive behaviors returned, like the hunt for female beauty and drowning the memories in liquor. Still,

### Gabriel Dimitri Jandaran

Arrogant (Vain)
Very Greedy (Hungers for money)
Loving (Understanding, accommodating)
Seasoned but doubting consultant/detective
Become debt-free (Burning)

> **Character Sheet (stats)** — *Attribute and skill values extracted from the printed character sheet; see the original PDF for the complete stat block.*

Lockpicking: Door locks (+2 Dexterity)
Instinct (+2 Intuition)
Good Shape (Endurance +1)
Rhetoric: Army (+2 Leadership)
Amateur Boxing (Defense: Melee +1, Endurance +1)
Weapon Handling: Pistol (+2 Firearms)
Combat Experienced (Overview +1)
Clear-Thinking: Tactics/strategy (+2 Reasoning)
Well-Trained (Size +1)
Observant: Notice deviations/details (+2 Awareness)
Situational Awareness (+2 Worldly)
Persuasive: Convince/beseech (+2 Persuade)

Notable weakness: Women (Beautiful women): Penalty (−2) (Sense) when activated
Significant fear: Dense fog/smoke/gas: Penalty (−5) (Resistance) when activated
Nightmares: Penalty (−5) (Sense) during Well-Being recovery

**Indebted (Completely):** 100% of Credit Rating utilized
Basic code of honor (Not harm women or children): If broken, Shocking experience
Sturdy (Size 21), height approx. 190 cm, weight approx. 90 kg
Standard of Living 6: 25,000 cash, Credit 250,000
Attractive appearance: Penalty/bonus (1) in social situations

**Hindrance (Sense):** Double experience cost when increasing
Charming: Impression +2
Tough: Endurance +2
Reputation: Noted (6): Underworld: Penalty/bonus (1) when used

In these abilities Gabriel is considered to have no competence (see page 213).

### What limitations and features Gabriel has, and their effects, are described briefly.

Expertises that give bonuses to abilities are written with the bonus first. Where expertises give increased attribute values, the number is instead written after the attribute, for greater clarity.

### After each ability, any learning marks are indicated (see page 221).

**Gabriel's Hindrance is marked with a ring around the box for learning marks.**

> **Character Sheet (form fields)** — *This section contains the fillable equipment, relationship and combat stats form. See the original PDF for the form layout.*

in the evenings, several times a week, he trains at the gym and boxes, partly to stay in shape, partly to process the assign-ments he's forced to carry out.
He's left several broken hearts in his wake. A man in his line of work rarely gets truly close friends and he pre-fers to keep to himself when given the chance. During his time as a freelancer he's seen much that's abhorrent and participated in both this and that — but it's part of the job. Soon the big assignment will come, the one that brings the big money, the one that means he can retire, move to a different, better part of the city. Any day now...
Step 15: Name (p. 29)
Marco decides to give his protagonist the name
Gabriel Dimitri Jandaran, where Dimitri is rarely used.
Gabriel Jandaran holds the rank of captain in the army. He is 34
years old.
Step 16: Cash & belongings (p. 29)
Since Gabriel's standard of living is (6) he receives 25,000
zovrins in cash. These can be used for purchasing be-longings if desired. Marco has Gabriel buy two Calder pistols, two shoulder holsters, a snub-nosed revolver and an ankle holster, ten extra magazines and 200 rounds for the pistols, 100 rounds for the revolver, a pump-action shotgun with 24 shotgun shells placed in a duffel bag, licenses for the weapons, a lockpick set and spare picks sewn into his
"official" tie, and finally a tape recorder, notepad, flashlight and a camera. This leaves him with just over 16,000 zovrins burning a hole in his wallet.
**Complete**
Now Gabriel Jandaran is ready to step into the exciting stories that Petter has planned for him and the other protagonists.
**Gabriel's family**
Note that in the background section it's stated that Gabriel has a father and a mother. But since they didn't crystallize during the selection of
Limitations (Family), it means they're irrelevant for the stories Marco wants to experience with
Gabriel. The point of relationships in general and blood and family relationships in particular is that they can be used as hooks for exciting stories. It's quite possible that Gabriel's parents later become relationships during a story through use of Fate (see Knows a contact on page 240).

The value here comes from the current Skill, its expertises (in this case Weapon Handling:
Pistols) and from the holster the weapon is usually carried in
(see page 119). Note that the pump-action shotgun
Rais H100 lacks Draw since Marco considers it highly unlikely it will ever be drawn in mechanical terms. If Gabriel uses the weapon, he has it in hand, and not concealed on his person.
**The value here comes from the current**
Skill and any expertises (in this case Weapon Handling: Pistols).
Here the Damage Increase is already added for faster handling.
The value here comes from the weapon and its holster, if there is one, or alternatively how the weapon is carried.
Malfunction indicates which numbers the dice must show in an exceptional failure for the weapon to malfunction. All of Gabriel's weapons are of normal quality/condition which means the normal outcome is required — doubles.

Archetypes &
background packages
An archetype is a general description of a person and their personality, typical knowledge and background.
Choose an archetype that seems interesting, exciting, challenging or perhaps easy to portray.
**Archetype description**
Each archetype is described with ideas, possibilities and selections for use and portrayal. The sections for nature and occupation provide more substance and the player can advantageously choose a concept (see page 27) from these.
A suggestion for how the attribute values for the arche-type might look is presented here below. Note that the player is free to swap between different attribute values, and that the given values are only recom-mendations that suit the chosen archetype. If the attribute values are changed, this also affects the ability values.
The archetype description also gives suggestions for which abilities are suitable for the archetype. The player is of course free to follow or ignore these recommen-dations, but the narrator should ensure no strange results occur, such as if a devoted commando soldier couldn't handle weapons.
If the player chooses a different set of abilities, or wishes to change the presented ability values in the archetype description, just follow the rules on page 28 for how ability values are acquired.
Under "Expertises" are shown suggestions for expertise areas that suit the archetype — but the player is of course free to change these. The expertise areas are based on the ability values given in the archetype de-scription. Note that if the player swaps out or changes ability values, perhaps certain expertises can no longer be chosen. See page 58 for more information about expertises.
**List of archetypes**

### Lawyer

"You misunderstand. I don't need to know whether you are a murderer or not, Mr. Timaran. All I need to know is what happened, who knows something about it, and that you can pay my fee."
A lawyer is a specialized type of bureaucrat whose task is to interpret the State's complicated legal system and use it to defend or prosecute individuals. Depending on the lawyer's connections, knowledge and influence, their price varies greatly. The very cheapest lawyers, who are appointed to those who have no money to pay with, are seldom capable.
But not even the most skilled lawyer can influence a legal case where the State or one of the families accuses a person, as they can modify the law to suit themselves, and the
State's special police, the commissars, have the right to pass sentence on the spot without any legal review whatsoever. Despite this, lawyers constitute an important professional group within which the skilled can twist the law to their advantage in most legal cases.
A lawyer has good insight into the state bureaucracy and its legal code—something that gives the lawyer great advantages in most bureaucratically difficult situations and makes them respected and feared. It is not uncommon for lawyers to take bribes to forget parts of the evidence or plant false evidence.
Despite their advantageous position, the lawyer runs great risk of ending up in dangerous situations if they become involved in criminal cases where the accused are powerful and dangerous individuals who don't shy away from threats and violence.
Likely nature:
- Corrupt
- honorable
- cunning
- driven
- knowledgeable
- incompetent
- bold
- arrogant
- insane
- cowardly
- dull
- colorful
- cynical
- bitter
- committed.
Likely occupation:
- Prosecutor
- defense attorney
- judge
- civil lawyer
- state lawyer
- military lawyer
- advisor or schnapps lawyer.

**Businessman**
"A first edition of Hellenov's 'Children of the Macabre
Dance'? Indeed, if you wait a moment, sir, I shall retrieve it from the safe. I'll just close up the shop first and fetch my son, so he can keep watch. It is a valuable item, as you surely already know."
A sharp mind, cold nerves and a strong will are required to survive as a businessman in Sandukar's world. Businessmen and -women constantly work to manage their operations, negotiate with suppliers and improve their selection and try to reach and retain customers. It's also necessary to know who must be bribed, who must not be offended and who to hire to handle problems.
If one is skilled, one can go far, but the more power and money one gets, the more hungry, envious eyes begin to watch. Some are content to keep their business at one and the same level while others strive to rise in society, with bold ventures and forceful expansion.
The higher up one comes, the more versed one must be in the bureaucratic system that governs society, for the latter can at any time turn against you and crush you if you don't follow the paragraphs and ordinances that have been declared.

- vain.

### Academic

"Doctor Villsayan, sir! Doctor! Come quickly, I have found the key! It was hidden between the sheets in the second bundle. It was chance that I discovered that the roughness of the paper was opposite when I ran my hand over it, and against the light. There I saw that the instructions were hidden! Do you understand? We have found the key!"
Among dusty tomes, old writings and contemporary discoveries, the academic tries to expand their knowledge. Those who don't accept the State's description of the world or history but try to create their own picture of reality walk very dangerous paths. To defy the State's censorship and search for a different truth than the one given means risking arrest and execution for espionage and terrorism. Research often requires experimentation, which makes the need for laboratories present. Sometimes horrible experiments are carried out to achieve new knowledge, often with suffering and injury as a result.
Horrific experiments occur in soundproofed rooms with steel gurneys and in attic rooms expeditions to forbidden ruined cities and lonely valleys are planned. In many academics the thirst for knowledge burns so strongly that they are prepared to sacrifice everything and defy all rules to find what they consider to be the truth.
Many academics choose, however, to follow the State's worldview and often support themselves as teachers, librarians, researchers or similar.
Likely nature:
- Shy
- manic
- intrusive
- ruthless
- insane
- curious
- boring
- interested
- dry
- knowledge-thirsty
- cowardly
- arrogant.
Likely occupation:
- Student
- librarian
- teacher
- instructor
- researcher
- scientist
- doctoral candidate
- museum curator
- psychologist
- profiler
- civil servant or bureaucrat.

**Worker**
"Wanna come grab a beer after the shift? The others were thinking of heading to Vaskarov's to listen to the match. The waitresses there aren't so uptight and the beer prices are affordable, damn, it's gonna get wild there. Tonight 'The Anvil' will show who's boss in the ring!"
Sandukar rests on the backs of workers. In long lines they toil in dangerous workplaces to produce what is needed for the city and empire to continue being driven forward.
They are parts in a machinery of flesh, gristle and bone.
Union control, workplace safety and rules about working conditions and hours barely exist in Sandukar. Wages are low and many have difficulty managing. However, there are those who have the luck to be employed by people who care about their staff and these can sometimes find contentment in their work and companionship with their coworkers.
For it is the community that binds the workers together.
The hard toil they share with their brothers and sisters. The shit-talking about the bosses and the low wages. The talk about dreams that will never be able to be fulfilled. For freedom is found only in one's fantasies, in simple pleasures and in family life. When the whistle sounds, the workers either head home to their families or stop by a smoky tavern to have a beer or two after the day's grind. Those who are born in worker families most often end up following in their parents' footsteps and sometimes entire families work at the same factory and drive the eternal machinery onward.
Likely nature:
- Bored
- resigned
- engaged
- lazy
- professional
- talkative
- stupid
- helpful
- crude
- villainous
- corrupt.
Likely occupation:
- Builder
- industrial worker
- transporter
- plumber
- electrician
- welder
- mechanic
- craftsman
- laborer
- airship pilot
- taxi driver or guard.

### Artist

"She's pretty beautiful, isn't she? And what a voice she has! And her body... The dress she's wearing, it's anything but discreet! Oh yes, many men and women surely lust after her and the facade she's created around herself. But deep down she's surely just as rotten as the rest of us."
By offering themselves, stepping into a role and exploiting their talents, the entertainer makes a living by evoking emotions in their audience. It could be anything from strong emotions like tragedy and fate-laden sorrow in theatrical epics to swinging song numbers at smoky nightclubs—a skilled enter-tainer should be able to produce both laughter and tears. With their presence on the silver screen, the stage or at the street corner, the entertainer tries to make the viewer forget, for a moment, the grey everyday reality, dream away and allow themselves to be seduced by his/her presence.
The big, popular names attract a loyal audience, big money and many privileges. However, the profession is difficult. Only the truly talented, those with good connections or those willing to do anything to achieve success have the opportunity to go far; others must struggle along with jobs on the side, and for them the competition is fierce. Many turn to drugs to cope with the pressure and some become completely con-sumed by their roles and burn out at a young age.

- Passionate
- serious
- depressed
- manic
- insane
- easygoing
- superficial
- vain
- exhibitionistic
- profound
- flamboyant
- withdrawn
- open-hearted
- stupid
- brilliant
- manipu-lative
- intense
- gloomy
- irresponsible
- arrogant
- burned out.
Likely occupation:
- Theater actor
- film actor
- musician
- singer
- comedian
- club entertainer
- varie-ty artist
- magician
- dancer
- poet
- model or mannequin.

**Athlete**
"296... 297... Come on now! 298. Two more. Then the punching bag, followed by the speed bag. 299. Don't be such a weakling! You've got another sit-up in you.
Yes, come on. That's it. That's it! Yes, 300! Well done, kid. One day it's you Mr. Rasparan will be interviewing on radio, before loads of listeners. One day, kid..."
The athlete chooses to specialize in one or several disci-plines, everything from fencing to track racing. Popular activities are track racing, boxing, running competitions, tennis, golf, rugby, basketball and rowing. High society favors polo or horse racing. The main thing is that the practitioner has devoted a large part of their time and dedication to a particular area to become as skilled as possible at it. Skilled athletes can achieve star sta-tus but most must struggle along and often have jobs on the side.
When success comes, shady promoters, managers and agents emerge from the shadows wanting to sign contracts, where they promise money, power, luxury life, wine, women and song—the only thing the athlete must do is win the next competition, and the next, and the next... There's always someone younger, more skilled, faster, more enduring or more daring waiting for the athlete to mess up, so they can take over.
Some choose to improve their results through drugs, bribes or cheating, but if discovered it can cost them dearly.

- burned out.

- football

- etcetera).

### Con Artist

"Of course I can help you Miss Adescu—I know the finest doctors when it comes to performing abortions painlessly and in secret. However, I must demand a favor in return but it's nothing big or dangerous we're talking about.
Here, there now, take my handkerchief, dry your tears and then tell me where your father keeps his notebook."
Through cunning, manipulation and blackmail the con artist gets what he/she desires. That lives are ruined and families torn apart along the way is met with little sympathy—it's the punishment the victims get for allowing themselves to be deceived.
Finding gullible victims' weaknesses and passions and then exploiting them to the fullest is the natural approach for a con artist. As well as finding the right individual for the con. It's precisely the ability to read individuals and the cold-bloodedness to treat them as objects that makes a con artist success-ful. Many become lost in the game with human emotions and find the hunt for control and humiliation as strong as the lust for wealth. There are con artists who have entire net-works of people under them, people they have leverage on, or who are completely captivated by them.

- Ice-cold
- smart
- calculating
- charismatic
- sadistic
- charming
- accommodating
- understanding
- appreciative
- flattering
- curious
- bold
- daring
- manipulative
- quick-witted.

- Charlatan
- snake-oil salesman
- swindler
- hustler
- fortune hunter
- opportunist
- womanizer
- seductress
- cult leader
- quack or blackmailer.

**Criminal**
"Since those bastards from the Hexagon came to the nest, nothing's the same anymore. They check up on everything and those who try to fool them turn up in the canal with a bullet lodged in their skull. Thanks, I remember the good old days when gangs were run by strong leaders who settled things with fists, knives or pistols—not like now when everything's run by corruption and double-crossing. Soon you can't bloody trust anyone anymore."
In a harsh existence controlled by the State and by a brutal and corrupt police force, crime often seems to many to be the only way up out of misery. People toil day and night at factories and still can't support their families. Selfish-ness takes over then and you let other people suffer so your own needs can be fulfilled. Maybe you engage in something as simple as pickpocketing, burglary and robbery or you deal with a type of criminal activity that has branches in many parts of the city.
Criminals form groups that can gain great influence.
Some such groups grow into power factors and some-times have great control in parts of the city. The sense of community born from sharing a common livelihood can become very strong. However, you must be careful not to anger the wrong people, regardless of which side of the law. But with bribes, threats and deals you can go far as long as you respect those above you.

### Bureaucrat

"So you wish to use our archives for the purpose of, what did you call it… Ahh yes, that's right, research for personal satisfaction. You must be aware, madam, that this is a violation of nearly every legal statute I as a state official follow. But… for a tidy sum of 25,000 zovrins I can arrange a temporary permit for you and your associates."

Sandukar's vast machinery requires its cogs and wheels. These hard-working individuals are the bureaucrats. They struggle with administration and upholding the laws and ordinances upon which the State rests. In massive buildings with lonely corridors and endless rows of filing cabinets, the bureaucrat toils to preserve an idea of a society he/she cannot understand or overview. The hierarchy is draconian and only through working obediently and faithfully for one's superiors can one hope to advance in importance and recognition.

There are, however, some who try to take shortcuts by keeping on good terms with people within the clans or smearing their colleagues—but these play a dangerous game. Actions that can lead them to influential positions in society can just as easily plunge them into ruin if their schemes are revealed. Thus most bureaucrats choose to go the legal and bureaucratic route but bend the rules just enough to make it as advantageous as possible for them.

- formal
- helpful
- smug
- pragmatic
- strict
- obedient
- dry

- vindictive
- scheming
- suspicious.

- archivist
- clerk
- courier

- controller
- auditor
- examiner
- coordinator or planner.

### Lone Wolf

"I've never felt comfortable among people, they're like vermin, stinking and infinite in number. Believe me, people are better off when I'm not in their vicinity. Ask those who now lie in the sewers overrun by rats—though on the other hand, one could say I merely returned them to their equals."

Among Sandukar's millions of people it's easy to be overlooked and abandoned. Many live in total isolation, don't know their neighbors or the people they work with, and no longer have contact with their families. Some seek this isolation and choose solitude over company. Perhaps they choose it because they enjoy their own thoughts and their own company. Perhaps they're afraid of hurting someone, or of the outside world gaining insight into their secrets.

Lone wolves usually support themselves through odd jobs, criminal activities, or by begging for money on the street. There are also those who have become scrap collectors—they take things people have thrown away, repair them, and sell them onward. Life on the street is very hard, nights are spent in abandoned houses, shabby apartments, or in seedy hotel rooms. As a lone wolf one is neither wanted nor missed, and the life one leads provides no good prospects for reaching old age.

It's not uncommon for the lone wolf to be a wanderer who moves through the city and stays only a short time in any one place.

Likely nature:
- Bitter
- cynical
- hopeful
- dejected
- sarcastic
- taciturn
- evasive
- restless
- insane
- aggressive
- self-destructive
- manic
- distrustful
- desperate.

Likely occupation:
- Vagabond
- outcast
- fugitive
- criminal
- unemployed
- hunter
- survival expert
- odd-jobber
- murderer
- mentally ill
- avenger or crime fighter.

### Femme Fatale

"Ah, my beloved, you came after all. Yes, I've packed and a car is already waiting down on the street. That we'll finally get to flee and start our own lives—together. You got hold of the family jewels, didn't you? Because without money we won't survive. Ah, you have them in the suitcase, excellent! Why I have a pistol in my hand? See it as a symbol that you are too gullible. There now, don't spill any tears, I'm not spilling any for you."

She knows what she wants and stops at nothing to get it. Manipulating, nagging, and begging are not beneath her. A femme fatale is a woman who has a goal and intends to achieve it at any cost. She does this through lies, threats, enticements, and sex—because these are paths that reach into the frozen heart of the men, or women, she manipulates into doing what she wants. It might involve getting rid of a competitor, murdering a spouse for inheritance and wealth, escaping a pursuer, or pulling off the perfect heist.

Some femmes fatales use manipulation to survive, others deceive and betray merely for their own pleasure. On the street and in criminal organizations women have learned the art of seduction to be able to work their way up, but must always be vigilant and cautious as it's a dangerous game they play. Among the clans, seduction is an art form and there it's more about cruel games. A woman who has learned to twist her victims around her fingers has a powerful tool at her disposal, and many times her manipulative charm can be just as deadly as a lead bullet as it often strikes the heart.

Likely nature:
- Ice-cold
- smart
- ensnaring
- calculating
- sadistic
- masochistic
- charming
- understanding
- appreciative
- flattering
- curious
- bold
- vulnerable
- manipulative
- quick-witted
- passionate
- sexy
- irresponsible.

Likely occupation:
- Housewife
- seductress
- thief
- fugitive
- model
- upper-class dame
- heist planner
- avenger
- crime victim
- madame or mistress.

### Fixer

"So what does my lord desire? Here with us no lusts are forbidden and no wishes too strange. Yes, I understand if your wish is something beyond the normal, otherwise you wouldn't be here, would you? So tell me, do you want a woman, a man, a child? Or is it perhaps drugs or weapons you crave? With us you can get everything—the question is only how much you're willing to pay."

Through contacts and colleagues the fixer can procure most things. He/she knows people in most agencies in society and knows where to go and whom to pump for information, equipment, or to have a particular type of service performed. An untraceable revolver, a police report obtained through back channels, information about who the film star slept with the night before, or smuggling someone out of the city are things the fixer can handle.

Many specialize in a specific area such as weapons, drugs, slaves, transport, information, or specific services. The fixer must be cautious, however, not to cross the wrong people, and bribing law enforcement is required for the fixer's operation to be left in peace.

Many fixers begin with time to see the entire social world as a collection of services and favors. If an acquaintance becomes too demanding these disillusioned individuals don't hesitate to terminate it or to sell their former friends to someone who offers some form of payment.

- superficial

- surly

- greedy
- spacey.

### Freelancer

"Okay, let me repeat the whole thing to make sure I understand you correctly. You want me to get to manufacturer Janis, execute him and his family in a way that makes your enemies not dare stand up against you again? Don't think it'll be a problem, but I'll demand half the payment upfront. When it comes to macabre assignments like this, I want to make sure I don't get cheated. After payment is complete, we'll never see each other again."

The freelancer is a person who goes their own way or lets themselves be hired for services or substantial sums of money. However, they want to be their own and avoid becoming too closely tied to a particular organization. Many times the freelancer is hired to carry out distasteful, illegal and brutal assignments only to disappear afterward. One can find freelancers in many parts of society. It could be anything from private detectives who don't hesitate to take morally questionable assignments to professional killers in the underworld who have made killing their art. Violence and death seem to seek out freelancers and many become notorious and feared. But life is hard and as if it weren't enough that the degenerate experiences risk tearing their minds apart, freelancers can at any moment be subjected to assassination attempts from former antagonists or employers.

### Street Vagrant

"Nadja doesn't live anymore, she went into the fallen tower to search for valuables and something attacked her. I heard her screams and rushed there but found only parts of her. So now one is alone again. Fortunately the bottle doesn't abandon one — it makes one endure another day in this hell!"

The street vagrant lives outside the system. They support themselves by begging, stealing and/or scavenging for food. Some perform simple services to scrape together enough money to get by. Most people don't even notice the pale and hollow-eyed figures sleeping in stairwells and alleys and wandering around like sleepwalkers. As a street vagrant you have no rights, no one will defend you or search for your killer if something goes wrong. However, the homeless see things more clearly than many others and among them are many hidden knowledges about Sandukar's forbidden areas, underground passages and modern myths.

The street vagrants live in many ways in their own world, in the shadow of humanity and the eternal city. There they wait, watch and fight for their survival. Among them one can find fallen prophets, mad leaders, visionaries and lunatics. They fight among themselves over food scraps from dumpsters and hide from the police's searchlights to avoid beatings or execution. Hunger, alienation and cheap liquor cause their psyche to break down and they have difficulty distinguishing dream from reality. Many die after only a few years on the street, frozen to death during winter nights or beaten to death in the search for something to eat.

- depressed

### Tactical Officer

"Now it's time to clean up here, let no one escape, show no mercy. Those who live here in the house are all society's scum, men, women, children and elderly. Like a cancer tumor they have grown and now threaten the realm's survival. We are the scalpel that cuts away the tumor and brings order! So men, ready your weapons, this morning traitor blood shall be spilled."

Tactical officers are cold-blooded and combat-ready individuals deployed when the police force expects heavy resistance or needs to clean up dangerous areas. They can almost be likened to a military force within the police. They are often considered lacking in finesse and have the mentality of shoot first and ask questions later, but in their profession this is usually a requirement to survive. Some are driven entirely by aggression and adrenaline during missions, others by a sense of bringing justice, but all are marked in one way or another by the violence. Constantly encountering desperate people seconds before they behold death wears on the peace of mind and many seek destructive paths to relax and release the pressure.

- corrupt

- burned out.

- riot police

- security troops
- state protection unit or escort force.

### Artist

"Good, insert the needle a bit deeper into her chest, I want to capture her suffering. Yes like that. Now, put the noose around her neck and pull tight. Don't worry, she's been my model many times before so I know what she can handle. Now you can let her get some air, I need to change the film in the camera."

The world is observed, filtered and recreated through the artist. Impressions are gathered through studies of human behavior, nature's hidden language and culture's strange choices. Some artists resort to drugs to gain greater insight and inspiration, others sink into suffering and depression in the hunt for something greater. Art becomes an interpretation of the present and the artist absorbs the essence their surroundings give off. It's mixed with their thoughts, fantasies and experiences and shaped into paintings, music and literary works.

These creative people rarely fit the societal mold and many have difficulty supporting themselves. State censorship also complicates free thinking and many let their creation happen in secret and expose themselves only to a smaller audience. Some artists specialize in the generally accepted and popular and can succeed in living off their profession even if the creative side isn't satisfied. Others let the works take macabre forms, difficult for many to understand, but somewhere in the incomprehensible truth and insight can reveal themselves.

- depressed
- melancholic
- trying

- spacey
- difficult
- talkative
- strange.

- photographer or poet.

### Cop

"So, are you going to tell me what you know? I know you're having trouble talking but trust me, it can get worse. A few knocked-out teeth are nothing compared to what I can do to you if I get really angry. So tell me now, who were your accomplices in the heist at the Golden Salon?
You don't seem to understand who you're messing with, punk. I AM
the law and the law doesn't care about scum like you."

As a cop you get to experience the violence, misery, terror, and the most unspeakable of perversions only to then return to your normal life, with family and colleagues who tackle the same terrible experiences. Not many manage this balancing act without being dragged down into depression and the feeling of helplessness. Many become desensitized and emotionally cold, others try to suppress the experiences through substance abuse. Being forced to experience the worst in all people can crush the strongest. However, there are those who keep their spirits up, don't let themselves be corrupted and actually see that their efforts make a difference, that they succeed in saving lives, helping fellow human beings and putting scoundrels behind bars. Yet it's hard to stay on the right side of the law, even for its guardians, especially when the criminals often have better resources, more money and contacts all the way up to the cabinet, the courts and the ministries.

### Prostitute

"If the gentleman wants to tie me up, spit on me and take me everywhere it costs two hundred. Expensive? I'm young, doll-like and good at what I do, unlike the other worn-out scabby whores down here. I know what I'm worth... and for you, here and now, that's two hundred."

The prostituted women and men, the girls and boys, live in harsh conditions with dirty brothels, dark alleys, dilapidated apartments and car front seats as their workplaces. Even though there are those who work their way up to higher levels with richer clients, the prostitutes' existence is based on being exploited for payment.
Many use drugs to numb the experiences and most are subordinated to pimps who take the greater part of their money and keep them in a firm grip.

Self-destructiveness and ever-expanding boundaries for which services are performed for payment lead to few prostitutes succeeding in leaving their profession. However, there are those who manage to establish a somewhat tolerable existence together with their sisters or brothers as family. A small clique that has been blessed with looks and aptitude may come to reach positions as kept mistresses for rich and/or famous individuals, and maintain their own apartment and pocket money.

### Priest

"In this world of total darkness you do right in coming to me, my child. There is much cruelty and horror and if one is not careful it drags one down into its perdition. Open your heart and tell of the deeds you have committed and for which sins you should be punished. Believe me
I have heard most things and the One forgives all."

In times of darkness and despair the church is an important point for the faithful. To spread the word of the One is a great responsibility that often weighs on the priest's mind. For how does one know that the words one spreads are the One's and not one's own? How does one avoid falling for sin oneself in a world that seems lost?
The State has in many ways made it more difficult for the church and made people afraid to seek comfort and support, but despite that the priest is an important individual who is respected in many parts of society.

The priests represent the ancient, a different perspective and the dream that somewhere beyond the human there is something greater. Beyond the cold and hard world there is joy and salvation for those who strive to work hard and do good. This very fact, that something would be greater and more powerful than the State, is in itself provocative for many. But people want to have something to believe in. They want hope.

### Reporter

"The article about how the top bosses at the Svakov factory ritually executed the workers who stood up against them caused me more trouble than benefit. Likewise the zeppelin scandal and the security at the mismanaged air harbor. I
have been threatened countless times, but someone has to get the truth out to the people. My editor has a thick letter with pictures and copied documents that he will open if I disappear or have an accident. So far the letter's existence has saved me from horrors, but the latest scoop is so big that I suspect the insurance won't be enough."

In a society where all news is under strict censorship, where complaints against the State can mean the death penalty, and where biased reporting constitutes the norm, the reporter fills an important function. Even if the news becomes biased, they give a view of the world that constitutes reality for Sandukar's millions of inhabitants. However, there are reporters who strive to get the truth out and who in secret radio broadcasts and underground newspapers fight to get their messages across. They risk torture and the death penalty if they are captured, as they are regarded as terrorists and insurrectionists.

A reporter has good insight into the part of the city he/
she is located in, and plenty of contacts and sources. Moreover, he/she has access to masses of archive material at the editorial office and stumbles now and then upon unpleasant conspiracies and strange events.

### Brawler

"You might not get it, but you're not getting in here! Not now, not ever. Are you completely fucking stupid in the head? You pulling a knife on me? Dumb bastard!"
The Brawler makes a living fighting for various reasons. Being constantly in brawls or messy situations is his/her life. It could be a way to make ends meet, for example through illegal boxing matches or death fights. Or it's just that the language of fists comes naturally to the person, perhaps due to long-term bullying during upbringing, chaotic conditions, petty crime, or other factors that created a need for the ability to defend oneself. The
Brawler's life is often marked by injuries and scars from fights, and he/she frequently suffers permanent damage, memory problems, or a smashed-in face. To numb the pain, many choose to turn to painkillers or alcohol.
Few brawlers manage to live a normal life. Some try to settle down, find honest work, and perhaps even start a family, but the violence in their past has left its mark and it's hard not to fall back into it. Enemies from before who suddenly appear, frustrating situations, and mockery from bosses or colleagues can be very difficult to ignore when the fists are so eager to speak. The Brawler wages a constant battle against himself, his animalistic side, and his own aggression. Violence seems to have taken root forever, and many brawlers eventually give themselves over to it completely.
Likely nature:
- Taciturn
- survivor
- bully
- aggressive
- single-minded
- stupid
- emotionally disturbed
- self-assertive
- sadistic
- brutal
- bitter
- burnt out
- disturbed
- driven.
Likely occupation:
- Boxer
- bouncer
- odd-jobs worker
- petty criminal
- laborer
- bodyguard
- enforcer
- unemployed
- or martial artist.

- Presence (3); Charm (1), Leadership (2), Persuade (3)
