**Soldier**
"The meal is served—a watery gruel with a piece of black bread. A luxury meal worthy of a minister. I shall make my way over to the dugout in the trench, so the rain doesn't water down the soup any more. The rifle is so heavy and cumbersome. Soon it's leave. I long for it so. Almost there. The fog seems to be lifting out over the mud field. Damn it, an incoming grenade attack. You learn to recognize that whistling sound in your sleep. If you sleep at all. There it detonated.
Cursed enemy, I hate you! Well great, just what I needed—mud splatter in my food. Oh well, there's an assault planned tonight so who needs to eat..."
The State needs fodder for war's meat grinders, and it's the soldiers who are devoured. As a soldier, you get lousy compensation for defending your country, your way of life, and your ideals against the enemy. Usually the circumstances are terrible.
Death stinks in the trenches while machine guns roar, gas grenades detonate, and orders are screamed. If the enemy's bullets don't finish off the soldier, the environment does its best, with starvation, diseases, infections, and madness. As a soldier, you've seen most of the worst that's hidden in man's interior.
Virtually no one who joins the army returns home without being changed. Some suffer from physical and psychological damage. Others are hardened and cold, and some long to return to the trenches where life is mapped out and simple when you just follow orders. Fellowship with people who haven't experienced war's horrors is difficult to maintain as you live in different worlds. Something in soldiers dies among barbed wire and minefields. Virtue is lost forever.
Likely nature:
- Idealist
- cynical
- sentimental
- war-damaged
- fearful
- slovenly
- heroic
- cold-blooded
- insane
- violent
- brutal
- calm
- broken
- patriotic.
Likely occupation:
- Private
- officer
- staff member
- intelligence agent
- courier
- specialist
- commando
- engineer
- strategist
- interrogator
- or pilot.

### Technician

"This electric clockwork should work perfectly, but for some reason it always runs slow toward morning when night withdraws. It's strange, somehow the water in the canals and the reservoir system seems to affect this mechanical construction..."
In Noir's industrialized society, maintenance is needed. It's handled by technicians who must ensure that machines work, that technology is available, and that everyone is satisfied.
The factories' machines are adjusted to function with utmost perfection. Reservoir dams in the canals are regulated and radio transmitters are installed. Cogwheels, levers, transformers, and generators are tools that keep society alive.
Without maintenance and the technicians' brains, Sandukar would be a silent and dead city where everything of steel and cables would stand still.
By using their hands and their minds, the technician can create fantastic mechanical inventions that can result in everything from brutal war machines to precise clockworks.
However, one must follow state laws and there are a handful of areas within technical research that must not be entered.
Crossing these boundaries means being counted as a terrorist and thus risking execution. Exactly why these areas are forbidden is not explained, but most people value their lives and follow the templates the State has provided for how technological innovations should look.
Likely nature:
- Fanatical
- goal-oriented
- gruff
- shy
- manic
- efficient
- perfectionist
- cheerful
- clumsy
- clever
- focused
- cowardly
- curious.
Likely occupation:
- Inventor
- constructor
- engineer
- repairman
- mechanic
- or locksmith.

**Enforcer**
"You don't understand who you're messing with, you bastard!
Do I have to break more of your ribs before you cough up the money? You're the one who gambled it away, you're the one who spits it back up! Don't give me that old story again, wife and kids, bah! Next time it's your wife I'll kick the shit out of, so you better pay up before the week's over. Then it'll be your kids. Rulov's generosity has limits, and you passed them long ago. So now you're crying, you weak bastard!"
When someone needs to be threatened, beaten, or killed, the enforcer is called in. This cold-hearted individual makes a living by destroying lives and collecting debts for payment. The enforcer usually has a criminal past and has usually grown up in a world of violence and lies. This has hardened him/her to the degree that he/she now no longer hesitates to perform the most distasteful acts for a sum of money. Often the enforcer works for an organization in the underworld, but he/she can also function as a freelancer who is contacted when needed.
Many enforcers are alcoholic brutes who've been forced into the only work they're capable of performing. They tell themselves they are without guilt as they only follow orders, but deep down many suffer. But there are also those who see killing as an art, who find pleasure in breaking ribs and squeezing people for money. These dangerous individuals have already stared down into the abyss and no longer fear its darkness.
Likely nature:
- Taciturn

- reliable
- dutiful
- polite
- goal-oriented
- attentive
- resourceful
- patient
- perfectionist
- sadistic

- honest
- dishonest.

- or assassin.

### Investigator

"When my flashlight illuminated the bloody symbols on the tiled wall, I realized I had found a new clue. Here, in this insane pattern, were the traces that would lead me to the murderer. He's luring me in and I'm now too deep in to resist, even though the investigation has officially been shut down. He's trying to tell me something, something I've missed. But I'll have to turn over every stone again and go through evidence and testimony one more time. I'll get you, you bastard!"
An investigator is a person who spends their time interpreting clues, solving problems and searching for answers and people. Exactly what profession the investigator has can vary, he/she can be a police captain, hired consultant, problem-solver or private detective. Many investigators work for private individuals or companies.
A corrupt police force that doesn't have the time or resources to conduct proper investigations, along with the need to have certain more illegal assignments carried out, means that the investigator fills an important function. Many assignments are simple and time-consuming, for example finding missing persons, finding evidence of infidelity and the like. However, it happens that the investigator stumbles into truly dangerous situations when trails turn out to lead to conspira-cies and high-level conflicts. Many investigators have a past in the police force (if they're not still there) and have contacts on the street.

**Caretaker**
"Rarely have I met such a twisted and dangerous person as Maryla. The first time she managed to trick me into releasing her and then attacked me with a scalpel — it was sheer luck that I survived. The second time she killed a caretaker and escaped, but now I keep her drugged so that she's compliant. The conversations we have give me a unique insight into her morbid world and
I cannot stop being fascinated by her thoughts."
Illness, madness and human suffering have always exis-ted and in Sandukar's society it exists in abundance. There are however those who have chosen to focus on helping the sick and vulnerable. As a caretaker one encounters many individuals some of whom carry permanent injuries to both body and soul. But despite the horrors caretakers encounter, there are many who feel satisfaction from seeing the fruit of their labor, the souls they've saved, the epidemics they've stopped. Others have given up in the face of the cruel world that constantly strikes the innocent, that takes the lives of young people needlessly, or where meaninglessly random violence strikes against the good.
Caretakers exist in all parts of society, among the families one finds skilled private physicians who run luxurious clinics and in the slums there are doctors who struggle to cure those in need or who squeeze money out of those who ask for help. Many have realized the importance of having contact with skilled caretakers, as this can mean life and death for those who have been seriously injured or ill.

### Background Packages

The background packages exist to offer the player a way to give their protagonist a complete profile with minimal time investment. Here a number of ready-made packages are presented in the form of various concepts. In each package there are limitations and characteristics, as well as charac-ter traits and relationships. The idea is that a player who doesn't want to micromanage the protagonist's background can quickly find and use a ready-made package that fits, or is close to, the intended concept.
In the packages below the point costs are included so the player can easily swap out parts of the package if a choice doesn't fit into his/her vision of the protagonist. All limitations and charac-teristics are described in detail on pages 80–87. In addi-tion there are suggestions for character traits (see page 224) and relationships (see page 209) that fit into the package. In those packages where the limitation Family is included, the family has been included among the relationships.
The names of the background packages are chosen to give an idea of what type of protagonist they would be suitable for. However, there are no problems whatsoever in modifying the packages or using them for other types of protagonists.
**Irresponsible Hedonist**

**Limitations**

Wanted: Minor crimes [1 point]
- Family: Two narrator characters [3 points]
- Fear (noticeable): Condemning mother [2 points]
- Vice (overwhelming): Gambling (various types of card games) [4 points]
**Characteristics**

- Fatalist [3 points]
- Size: Small [1 extra point]
- Lucky: Luck 6 [6 points]
- Fate's Favorite [2 points]
**Character Traits**
Loving (passionate)
Indifferent (uninterested)

**Relationships**

Mother; Influence 18, Disapproving relationship, Blood tie
Son/daughter; Influence 6, Benevolent relationship, Blood tie
Doorman; Influence 9, Hostile relationship
Fixer; Influence 12, Disapproving relationship
Nightclub owner; Influence 15, Neutral relationship, Att-raction

"I prefer that you not buy me a drink."
"Why not?"
"I am a married woman."
"And that means?"
"That I do not seek your company."
"Then you would have said you were a happily married woman."
— Marlena Illianescu and
**Gabriel Jandaran**

What separates those who know from those who think they know is that the former possess practical experience, tricks and insights that amateurs cannot use through improvisation. This deeper understanding of, and insight into, a subject is collectively called expertise, and it gives the user clear advantages in situations where the expertise can be applied.
**Areas of Expertise**
A person can possess expertise in an ability. This means that the knowledge or skill in the ability is directed toward a specific subject, a specific maneuver or a specific situation, thus a narrower delimited subject within the broad ability.
Next to each ability are suggestions for areas of expertise, but nothing prevents a narrator from inventing more.

Expertise often gives a bonus to a roll if its conditions are met. For example, Weapon handling
(Automatic pistol) is an area of expertise for the ability
Firearms — when a person who possesses the expertise shoots with a pistol he/she gets a bonus, but if the person shoots with a shotgun instead the expertise's effect does not count. Areas of expertise have no value of their own that is increased but either you have them or not.

A person who gets a bonus from several different areas of expertise adds these together. This can mean that certain combinations of expertise can give high bonuses, which is the intention.

Unless otherwise stated, the general rule is that an area of expertise can only be chosen once.

Complexity & Requirements
Areas of expertise have two values beyond the effect:
complexity and requirements.

Complexity shows how difficult and comprehensive the area of expertise is. A person can at most possess areas of expertise whose total sum in complexity is equal to or lower than the person's ability value in the current ability. Complexity also shows how much experience the expertise costs, see page 222.

Requirements show what minimum ability value is required in the ability for a person to be able to learn the expertise.
If the person's ability value does not reach the value under requirements then the person cannot learn the expertise. Other requirements may also be listed, such as that the person must possess expertise in another field, for example.

**Expertises**

### Determination

Alert [3]
The person has a natural ability to learn things from their surroundings.
Requirements: Determination 7+, Quick learner
Rule: Increase the person's Learning by one (+1).
"It's just a flesh wound..." [3]
The person does not accept that he/she is mortally wounded.
Requirements: Determination 7+
Rule: If the person succeeds with a difficulty roll (Determination) with penalty (−5) he/she gets to reroll a failed difficulty roll (Will to live), when he/she has just been mortally wounded. The attempt increases the person's Stupor by the value of the highest die, regardless of whether the roll succeeds or not.
Stubborn [2]
The person refuses to give up.
Requirements: Determination 7+
Rule: If the person succeeds with a difficulty roll (Determination) with penalty (−5) he/she gets to make a new attempt to carry out an action that just failed. The expertise can only be used with a failed difficulty roll, not with resistance rolls, and may only be used once per game session.
Stubborn as sin [3]
The person really does not accept that he/she is mortally wounded.
Requirements: Determination 10+, "It's just a flesh wound..."
Rule: If the person succeeds with a difficulty roll (Determination) with penalty (−2) he/she gets to reroll a failed difficulty roll (Will to live), when he/she has just been mortally wounded. The attempt increases the person's Stupor by the sum of the value of all dice, regardless of whether the roll succeeds or not.
Focus [2]
The person can effectively shut out disturbing elements from the surroundings.
Requirements: Determination 4+
Rule: With a successful difficulty roll (Determination) the person gets for as many rounds as the value of the lowest die to ignore penalties from external influence (for example rain, blinding light, disturbing sounds, etcetera.) on the task that is the subject of the focus. Each attempt to focus wears on the person, who receives as much Stupor as the value of the highest die.
High-performing [3]
The person has a natural ability to learn things from their surroundings.
Requirements: Determination 10+, Alert
Rule: Increase the person's Learning by one (+1).
Quick learner [2]
The person has a natural ability to learn things from their surroundings.
Requirements: Determination 4+
Rule: Increase the person's Learning by one (+1).
Obstinate [4]
The person does not know what giving up is.
Requirements: Determination 13+, Persistent
Rule: If the person succeeds with a difficulty roll (Determination) he/she gets to make a new attempt to carry out an action that just failed. The expertise can only be used with a failed difficulty roll, not with resistance rolls, and may only be used once per scene.
Persistent [3]
The person really refuses to give up.
Requirements: Determination 10+, Stubborn
Rule: If the person succeeds with a difficulty roll (Determination) with penalty (−2) he/she gets to make a new attempt to carry out an action that just failed. The expertise can only be used with a failed difficulty roll, not with resistance rolls, and may only be used once per game session.
Endure strain [3]
The person manages strains through willpower.
Requirements: Determination 10+
Rule: Increase the person's Will to live by one (+1).
"Inside the courthouse you make sure to keep your mouth shut, don't say a word, don't react, don't let yourself be provoked. If you get a direct question then just answer
'yes' or 'no' and always add 'Your Honor'. If you manage to keep calm you might avoid the Fort. You got yourself into this mess, but you did right in hiring me, because I am the best you can get.
Now you do your part and I'll do mine. Hopefully we can shake hands outside the halls of justice after this, rather than you shaking bars."
— Franko Millovy
**Handling**
This section is laid out so that the summary of the expertises is on pages
60–61 and are sorted by requirements, while the reference section is sorted alphabetically for each ability. The idea is to first use the table to see which expertises a protagonist can acquire at all, given the different ability values he or she has.

Then use the reference section to get more detailed information about each expertise.

To directly use the reference section to see the connections and links between different expertises can easily seem unmanageable — then use the table for overview.

**List of All Expertises**

**Requirements:** Empathy 10+, Tangible Reading
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls
(Empathy) when using tangible reading. This bonus is added to other bonuses.

### Intuitive [4]

The person can put themselves in other people's situations, analyze them from their perspective, and can draw conclusions so real that they appear lifelike in the per-son's mind.

**Requirements:** Empathy 7+
**Rule:** The person finds it easy to subconsciously sense things about other people and can put themselves in others' situa-tions in an eerily effective way. This understanding is often very intense and fraught with anxiety. By reading criminal records, psychological evaluations, police reports, and inter-view summaries, the person can form a picture of another person's habits, likely behaviors, underlying drives, and motives. When an intuitive person who has studied a case thoroughly visits a crime scene or communicates with a suspect, the person may receive inner fragmentary im-ages of events that have occurred. These images often appear very frightening and tangible.

To receive such images requires the person to succeed with a difficulty roll (Empathy), with a penalty (−5). If the roll succeeds, the person receives a bonus (+2) in all forms of communi-cation with a chosen individual whom the person knows through study. This bonus applies primarily to dice rolls (Empathy)
but also to other forms of communication. The person may also use the bonus for dice rolls (Resist) in situa-tions where the chosen individual is present. The bonus is also applied to dice rolls (Deduction) related to a case in which the person is well-versed.

### Tangible Reading [2]

The person is very receptive to subconsciously interpreting people and understanding their state of mind.

**Requirements:** Empathy 7+, Reading
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls
(Empathy) when using reading. This bonus is added to the previous bonus.

### Sympathetic [2]

The person is perceived as sympathetic.

**Requirements:** Empathy 7+, Understanding
**Rule:** The person's Impression value increases by one (+1).

### Comfort & Calm [2]

The person possesses an ability to calm and comfort sad and upset people.

**Requirements:** Empathy 4+
**Rule:** The person receives a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Empathy)
when attempting to calm or comfort other individuals.

### Dexterity

**Experienced Pickpocket [2]**
The person is very skilled at performing a specialized form of pickpocketing.

**Requirements:** Dexterity 7+, Pickpocket (within the form)

### Charm

**Exceptionally Charismatic [3]**
The person is a specialist within their sphere.

**Requirements:** Charm 10+, Very Charismatic (within the sphere)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls
(Charm) within a sphere where they are very charismatic.
This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per sphere.

### Charismatic [2]

The person is knowledgeable within their sphere.

**Requirements:** Charm 4+
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls
(Charm) within a sphere they master. Choose one of the following: Act/Perform, Deceive/Manipulate, Seduce/
Captivate, Mingle/Converse, Sing/Play, Flatter/
Fawn, Entertain/Amuse. Write down which sphere the person masters well. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per sphere.

### Very Charismatic [2]

The person is experienced within their sphere.

**Requirements:** Charm 7+, Charismatic (within the sphere)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls
(Charm) within a sphere where they are charismatic. This bonus is added to the previous bonus. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per sphere.

### Empathy

**Reading [2]**
The person is receptive to subconsciously interpreting peo-ple and understanding their state of mind.

**Requirements:** Empathy 4+
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls
(Empathy) when it comes to noticing what state of mind a person is in.

### Animal Friend [3]

Animals do not attack the person as they possess a natural aura of calm and respect that animals perceive.

**Requirements:** Empathy 4+
**Rule:** The person rolls a difficulty roll (Empathy) to keep the animal calm. This roll receives a bonus (+5) and the chosen animal is calm for as many minutes as the lowest die value. If the animal is extremely aggressive or affected by, for example, drugs, the person can only keep it calm for a short while. In that case, the animal stays calm for as many rounds as the lowest die value.

### Understanding [2]

The person's understanding manner makes individuals around them feel at ease.

**Requirements:** Empathy 4+
**Rule:** The person's Impression value increases by one (+1).

### Intense Reading [4]

The person is extremely receptive to subconsciously interpreting people and understanding their state of mind.

**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Dex-terity) for a special form of pickpocketing where the person is already a pickpocket. This bonus is added to the previous bon-us. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per form.

### Pickpocket [2]

The person is skilled at performing a specialized form of pick-pocketing.

**Requirements:** Dexterity 4+
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Dex-terity) for a special form of pickpocketing they master. Choose one of the following: Planting, Stealing rings/
jewelry/watches, Stealing from handbags/pockets. Write down which form the person masters. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per form.

### Illusionist [3]

The person is a very skilled magician.

**Requirements:** Dexterity 10+, Magician (within the area)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Dex-terity) within an area where they are already a magi-cian. This bonus is added to other bonuses. The exper-tise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per area.

### Improvised Lockpicking [3]

The person is skilled at using improvised objects to open locks.

**Requirements:** Dexterity 7+, Locksmith (within the lock type)
**Rule:** The person receives no penalties when using improvised objects, such as hairpins, paper clips, or drinking glasses (for combination locks), to pick locks.

### Lockpicking [2]

The person can open locks without access to a key or combination.

**Requirements:** Dexterity 4+
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Dex-terity) to open a certain type of lock. Choose one of the following lock types: Car Lock, Door Lock, Padlock, Combination Lock, Security
Lock. Write down which lock type the person masters. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per lock type.

### Locksmith [2]

The person is skilled at opening locks without access to a key or combination.

**Requirements:** Dexterity 7+, Lockpicking (within the lock type)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Dex-terity) to open a type of lock they can already pick. This bonus is added to the previous bonus. The exper-tise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per lock type.

### Lock Specialist [3]

The person is a specialist at opening locks.

**Requirements:** Dexterity 10+, Locksmith (within the lock type)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Dex-terity) to open a certain type of lock where they are a locksmith. This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per lock type.

### Masterful Pickpocket [3]

The person is classified as a masterful pickpocket regarding a spe-cial form of pickpocketing.

**Requirements:** Dexterity 10+, Experienced Pickpocket (within the for-m)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Dex-terity) for a special form of pickpocketing where they are already an experienced pickpocket. This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times but only once per form.

### Tricks [1]

The person is versed in some magic tricks.

**Requirements:** Dexterity 4+
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Dex-terity) within an area they master. Choose one of the following areas: Distraction, Conceal in/produce from hand,
Card tricks/Coin tricks, Steady hands. Write down which area the person masters. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per area.

### Magician [2]

The person is an experienced conjuror.

**Requirements:** Dexterity 7+, Tricks (within the area)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Dex-terity) within an area where they master tricks.
This bonus is added to the previous bonus. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per area.

### Vehicle

**Experienced Driver [2]**
The person is skilled at operating and maneuvering a type of vehicle.

**Requirements:** Vehicle 7+, Driver (within the vehicle type)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Vehi-cle) for maneuvering a type of vehicle where they are already a driver. This bonus is added to the previous bon-us. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per vehicle type.

### Driver [1]

The person can operate and maneuver a type of vehicle.

**Requirements:** Vehicle 4+
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Vehi-cle) for maneuvering a certain type of vehicle. Choose one of the following vehicle types: Car, Bicycle, Canoe, Horse, Horse-drawn wagon, Truck, Motorboat, Motorcycle, Sailboat, Tank.
Write down which vehicle type the person masters. The exper-tise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per vehicle type.

### Train Engineer [1]

The person can operate and maneuver a locomotive.

**Requirements:** Vehicle 4+
**Rule:** The person knows how to control and handle a locomotive with accompanying cars. Without this know-ledge, the person can neither start nor operate a locomotive whatsoever.

### Additional Expertise Areas

Nothing prevents a player and narrator from creating new expertise areas together. Try to balance them based on the existing expertise areas so they don't become too weak or too powerful.

### Airship Pilot [1]

The person can operate and maneuver a zeppelin.

**Requirements:** Vehicle 4+
**Rule:** The person knows how to control and handle a zeppelin. Without this knowledge, the person can neither start nor maneuver a zeppelin whatsoever.

### Master Driver [3]

The person is very skilled at operating and maneuvering a type of vehicle.

**Requirements:** Vehicle 10+, Experienced Driver (within the vehicle type)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Vehi-cle) for maneuvering a type of vehicle where they are already an experienced driver. This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per vehicle type.

### Pilot [2]

The person can fly an aircraft type.

**Requirements:** Vehicle 4+
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Vehicle)
for maneuvering a certain type of aircraft. Choose one of the fol-lowing types: Small aircraft, Medium aircraft, Large air-craft. Write down which type the person masters. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per aircraft type.

### Captain [1]

The person can operate and maneuver a vessel.

**Requirements:** Vehicle 4+
**Rule:** The person knows how to pilot and operate a vessel.
Without this knowledge, the person can neither start nor maneuver a vessel whatsoever.

### Veteran [3]

The person is an experienced aviator of a certain aircraft type.

**Requirements:** Vehicle 7+, Pilot (within the aircraft type)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Vehi-cle) for maneuvering an aircraft type where they are already a pilot. This bonus is added to the previous bonus.
The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per aircraft type.

### Ace [3]

The person is an expert at flying.

**Requirements:** Vehicle 10+, Veteran (within the aircraft type)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Vehi-cle) for maneuvering an aircraft type where they are already a veteran. This bonus is added to other bonuses.
The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per aircraft type.

### Humanities

**Academic Authority [3]**
The person is a specialist within a field.

**Requirements:** Humanities 10+, Academic Specialist (within the field)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Humani-ties) within a field where they are an academic specialist.
This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be cho-sen multiple times, but only once per field.

### Academic Focus [2]

The person is knowledgeable within a subject or field.

**Requirements:** Humanities 4+
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Humani-ties) within a field they master. Choose one of the fol-lowing: Administration/Organization, Archaeology/Anthropology,
Economics/Bookkeeping, Philosophy, Geography/History, Heraldry/Semi-otics, Law/Political Science, Art History/Literary Studies,
Criminology/Profiling, Linguistics, Psychology/Sociology, Reli-gion/Occultism. Write down which field the person masters well.
The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per field.

### Academic Specialist [2]

The person is experienced within a field.

**Requirements:** Humanities 7+, Academic Focus (within the field)
**Rule:** The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Humani-ties) within a field where they have an academic focus. This bonus is added to the previous bonus.
The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per field.

### Linguist [2]

The person masters a foreign language.

**Requirements:** Humanities 4+
**Rule:** All people can speak, read, and write their mother tongue without problems. This expertise provides both oral and written knowledge in a foreign language. The languages that exist in the Imperium are described on page 170. The expertise can be cho-sen multiple times as long as a new language is chosen.

### Intuition

**Touch [2]**
The person knows how to interact with others so that they feel good.

**Requirements:** Intuition 4+
**Rule:** The person's Impression value increases by one (+1).

### Inspiration [2]

The person's subconscious helps them when danger is afoot.

**Requirements:** Intuition 4+
**Rule:** Increase the person's Awareness by one (+1).

### Instinct [2]

The person listens to their inner voice and gut feeling to receive guidance in life and to make decisions.

**Requirements:** Intuition 4+
**Rule:** When the person visits a place or finds themselves in a certain situation and succeeds with a difficulty roll (Intuition)
with a bonus (+2), they can sense if something is not right. It is just a feeling and provides no concrete information about the place/situation.

### Sense of Direction [1]

The person has a good sense of direction.

**Requirements:** Intuition 4+
**Rule:** The person always knows which direction north is and finds it easy to find their way and orient themselves. They often see a three-dimensional map before their mind's eye, with the places, areas, and streets they want to find.

"Something's wrong" [2]
The person is subconsciously vigilant to threats and reacts with lightning speed.
Requirement: Intuition 7+, Instinct
Rule: If the person is surprised in a combat situation (see page
254), the person automatically gets to make a difficulty roll (Intu-ition). If the roll succeeds, the person is not considered surprised.
See through person [3]
The person has an easy time detecting whether another person is lying or not.
Requirement: Intuition 7+, Instinct
Rule: The person makes a resistance roll (Intuition) with a bonus (+2). If the roll succeeds, the person can determine if an indi-vidual is lying or not. The expertise cannot be used to see exactly what the lie consists of, but gives a general feeling that the individual is being dishonest.
Self-preservation instinct [3]
The person has an instinctive sense of self-preservation.
Requirement: Intuition 7+, Instinct
Rule: Increase the person's Defense by one (+1).
Sixth sense [4]
The person has developed a sixth sense that warns when danger is present.
Requirement: Intuition 10+, Vigilant
Rule: The person gets a bonus (+5) on rolls (Intuition) when it comes to generally detecting danger. This bonus is added to the others from eyes in the back of your head and vigilant.
Vigilant [3]
The person has developed an uncanny ability to escape danger by trusting their instincts.
Requirement: Intuition 7+, Eyes in the back of your head
Rule: The person gets a bonus (+3) on rolls (Intuition) when it comes to detecting danger. This bonus is added to the one from eyes in the back of your head.
Eyes in the back of your head [2]
The person is in the habit of keeping track of their surroundings and not letting themselves be surprised.
Requirement: Intuition 4+
Rule: The person is vigilant by nature, and has a sense of whether danger lurks nearby. Often this is a subtle feel-ing that can manifest in the hairs on the neck standing up, the throat becoming dry, or similar. Exactly what constitutes the danger the per-son cannot determine and the sense is not entirely reliable. The per-son gets a bonus (+2) on rolls (Intuition) to notice when someone is following or intends to surprise him/her.
**Throwing Weapons**
Draw and throw [3]
The person can draw and throw a weapon in one and the same motion.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 7+, Throwing Specialist (within weapon type)
Rule: If the person has an Initiative of ten or higher and successfully manages to draw a throwing weapon he/she is a throwing specialist with, the person immediately gets to execute the Initiative action
Throw object at target, with the same weapon in the same
Initiative. This attack gets a penalty (−5) and lowers the person's
Initiative by five (−5) after the attack action, in addition to any initiative modifications that come from the Ini-tiative action's outcome.
Killing throw [4]
The person is deadly with throwing weapons.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 13+, "Direct hit" (Throwing Specialist within weapon type)
Rule: If the person succeeds in executing the Initiative action
Throw object at target with a weapon he/she is a throwing spe-cialist with, he/she gets to add 1D to the damage in addition to the extra damage for "direct hit".
Double throw [3]
The person can throw two throwing weapons in a single motion.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 10+, Throwing Specialist (within weapon type)
Rule: The person can use two already drawn throwing weapons simultaneously. When the person uses the Initiative action Throw object at target, he/she gets to add together the throwing weapons' dam-age. The attack gets a penalty (−5). This expertise can only be executed if the person is a throwing specialist with the weapons used.
Dual targets [3]
The person is expert at throwing at two different targets with one throwing weapon in each hand.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 13+, Double throw (throwing specialist within weapon type)
Rule: When the person uses the Initiative action Throw object at target with throwing weapons he/she is a throwing specialist with, he/she can choose to hit two targets. The thrower rolls only once, with a penalty (−5). Both targets get to defend themselves, and if anyone is damaged, the damage value is counted as being the weapon's normal, without any additional modification.
"Direct hit" [3]
The person knows how to hit to do great damage.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 7+, Throwing Specialist (within weapon type)
Rule: If the person succeeds in executing the Initiative action
Throw object at target with a weapon he/she is a throwing spe-cialist with, he/she gets to add 1D to the damage.
Throwing Specialist [2]
The person is expert at handling one type of throwing weapon.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 7+, Throwing Technique (within weapon type)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+3) when he/she uses a throwing weapon he/she can already handle. Write down which weapon type the person is expert with. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per weapon type.
Throwing Technique [2]
The person is skilled at handling one type of throwing weapon.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 4+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+2) when he/she uses a specific type of throwing weapon (for example Throwing Knife or Hand grenade). Write down which weapon type the person handles. The exper-tise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per weapon type.
Note that the bonus applies to all combat actions where the weapon is an active part – from drawing that weapon to throwing it.

Rule: If the person becomes mortally wounded, he/she gets five extra rounds before he/she dies. These rounds are added together with those from hard to kill and very hard to kill.
Fantastically good shape [4]
The person is in fantastically good shape.
Requirement: Endurance 13+, Exceptionally good shape
Rule: Increase the person's Stamina by one (+1).
Good shape [2]
The person is in good shape.
Requirement: Endurance 4+
Rule: Increase the person's Stamina by one (+1).
March [1]
The person is well-drilled in marching.
Requirement: Endurance 4+
Rule: The person gets a bonus (+2) when he/she uses
Endurance to march, see page 243.
Very good shape [2]
The person is in very good shape.
Requirement: Endurance 7+, Good shape
Rule: Increase the person's Stamina by one (+1).
Very hard to kill [3]
The person is very hard to kill.
Requirement: Endurance 10+, Hard to kill
Rule: If the person becomes mortally wounded, he/she gets three extra rounds before he/she dies. These rounds are added together with those from hard to kill.
Hard to kill [2]
The person is hard to kill.
Requirement: Endurance 7+
Rule: If the person becomes mortally wounded, he/she gets two extra rounds before he/she dies.
Swimming [1]
All people are assumed to be able to swim, but this person is competent in the water.
Requirement: Endurance 4+
Rule: The person gets a bonus (+2) on activities in water.
Sprint [1]
The person can effectively sprint short distances.
Requirement: Endurance 4+
Rule: Increase the person's combat movement by one (+1).
Strong lungs [2]
The person has good lung capacity.
Requirement: Endurance 7+
Rule: The person can hold their breath twice as long as a normal person.
Take it like a man [4]
The person has a tough physique and can handle hits that would have downed other people.
Requirement: Endurance 10+
Rule: Increase the person's Wound Threshold by one (+1).
Masterful sidestep [3]
The person can effectively defend against ranged attacks.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 13+, Sidestep
Rule: Increase the person's Defense against ranged attacks by two (+2).
Precision throw [2]
The person can throw an object with great accuracy.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 7+, Throwing Technique (within weapon type)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on the Initiative action
Throw object to location when he/she uses a weapon type he/she already has throwing technique in.
Sidestep [3]
The person can use their knowledge to avoid being hit.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 7+
Rule: Increase the person's Defense against ranged attacks by one (+1).
Combat ready [2]
The person is versed in how combat works.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 4+
Rule: Increase the person's Overview by one (+1).
Evade [2]
The person sacrifices their offensive capacity to avoid attacks.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 4+
Rule: The person can choose to give themselves a bonus to De-fend against a ranged attack. Before he/she rolls their resistance roll, he/she can choose to lower their Initiative by any number of steps, though at lowest to six. Each step the Initiative is lowered gives a bonus (+1) to the resistance roll's result.
Repetition [2]
The person can after a throw draw a weapon in the same motion.
Requirement: Throwing Weapons 10+, Draw and throw
Rule: If the person has an Initiative of ten or higher and successfully manages to execute the Initiative action Throw ob-ject at target, the person immediately gets to attempt to draw a new throwing weapon of the same type as the one just thrown. This attempt gets a penalty (−5) and lowers the person's Initiative by five (−5)
after the attack action, in addition to any initiative modifica-tions that come from the Initiative action's outcome.
**Endurance**
Diving [2]
The person can without problem stay and act underwater.
Requirement: Endurance 4+, Swimming
Rule: The person gets a bonus (+2) on activities while diving.
Exceptionally good shape [3]
The person is in exceptionally good shape.
Requirement: Endurance 10+, Very good shape
Rule: Increase the person's Stamina by one (+1).
Extremely hard to kill [3]
The person is extremely hard to kill.
Requirement: Endurance 13+, Very hard to kill

### Construction

Technical authority [3]
The person is a specialist within an area.
Requirement: Construction 10+, Technical specialist (area)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Con-struction) within an area where he/she is already a technical specialist. This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per area.
Technical focus [2]
The person is good at building and constructing one type of thing.
Requirement: Construction 4+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Con-struction) within an area he/she masters. Choose one of the following fields: Alarm systems, Bridges, Electrical objects,
Vehicles, (Craft), Houses, Throwing weapons, Camp setup,
Machines/Motors, Mechanical objects, Melee weapons,
Metalwork, Plumbing, Firearms, Carpentry, Explosives.
Write down which area the person masters. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per field.
Technical specialist [2]
The person is technically experienced within an area.
Requirement: Construction 7+, Technical focus (area)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Con-struction) within an area where he/she already has a tech-nical focus. This bonus is added to the previous bonus.
The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per area.
**Leadership**
Animal trainer [2]
The person can train and handle a certain animal type.
Requirement: Leadership 4+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Leader-ship) to train, control or handle an animal within one of the following types: Dog, Cat, Bear, Horse, Snake, Bird, Mouse/
Rat. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per animal type.
Control [3]
Through their controlling streak, the person is good at directing people within a group and getting them to obey.
Requirement: Leadership 10+, Presence (group)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Leader-ship) to direct and command, organize and decide within a group where he/she already has presence. This is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per group.
Brilliant pedagogue [3]
The person can get students to perform better.
Requirement: Leadership 10+, Train
Rule: If the teacher succeeds with a roll (Leadership), his/her students temporarily get to add five (+5) to their
Learning during the current study period. This bonus is added to other bonuses from teach and train.

Presence [2]
The person's presence affects those around them.
Requirement: Leadership 4+
Rule: The person's Impression value increases by one (+1).
Gravitas [2]
Through their natural gravitas, the person is skilled at controlling people within a group and getting them to obey.
Requirement: Leadership 7+, Rhetoric (group)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Leadership)
for directing and organizing, managing and commanding within a group where they already have rhetoric. This bonus is added to the previous bonus. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per group.
Commanding Respect [2]
The person has an aura of respect around them.
Requirement: Leadership 7+, Presence
Rule: The person's Impression value increases by one (+1).
Rhetoric [2]
Through their eloquence, the person is skilled at controlling people within a group and getting them to obey.
Requirement: Leadership 4+
Rule: The person gains a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Leadership)
for directing and organizing, managing and commanding within one of the following groups: Academic World,
General Public, Children, Crime Syndicates, Bureaucrats, Refugees,
The Streets, Club Scene, Criminals, The Church, Resistance Groups,
Military, Entertainment Industry, Police, Press, Justice System,
High Society, Sports World, The State, Clans. Write down which group the person controls within. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per group.
Intimidating [2]
Through their dominating aura, the person can frighten others.
Requirement: Leadership 7+, Rhetoric
Rule: The person can roll a dice roll (Leadership) to act threateningly toward people, instead of using Convince.
Teach [2]
The person can make students perform better.
Requirement: Leadership 4+
Rule: If the teacher succeeds with a roll (Leadership), their students temporarily add two (+2) to their
Learning during the current study period.
Educate [2]
The person can make students perform better.
Requirement: Leadership 7+, Teach
Rule: If the teacher succeeds with a roll (Leadership), their students temporarily add three (+3) to their Learning during the current study period. This bonus is added to the bonus from teach.
**Medicine**
Medical Authority [3]
The person is a specialist within a medical field.
Requirement: Medicine 10+, Medical Specialist (field)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Medicine)
within a field where they are already a medical specialist.
This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per field.
Medical Focus [2]
The person is knowledgeable within a medical field.
Requirement: Medicine 4+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Medicine)
within one of the following fields: Anatomy/Surgery/Forensic Medicine,
Diagnosis/Healthcare, Pharmaceuticals/Toxicology, First Aid,
Psychiatry/Hypnosis, Dentist, Veterinarian. Write down which area the person masters. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per field.
Medical Specialist [2]
The person is experienced within a medical field.
Requirement: Medicine 7+, Medical Focus (field)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Medicine)
within a field where they already have a medical focus.
This bonus is added to the previous bonus. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per field.
**Resist**
Exceptionally Perceptive [3]
The person has an exceptionally easy time seeing through social influence.
Requirement: Resist 10+, Very Perceptive (type of influence)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Resist)
for seeing through a type of influence where they are already very perceptive. This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per type of influence.
Perceptive [2]
The person has an easy time seeing through social influence.
Requirement: Resist 4+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Resist)
for seeing through any of the following: Seduction, Interrogation
Techniques, Threats, Bribery Attempts, Flattery, Persuasion. Write down which type of influence the person easily sees through. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per type of influence.
Very Perceptive [2]
The person has a very easy time seeing through social influence.
Requirement: Resist 7+, Perceptive (type of influence)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Resist)
for seeing through a type of influence where they are already perceptive. This bonus is added to the previous bonus. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per type of influence.
**Natural Science**
Scientific Authority [3]
The person is an authority within a scientific subject.
Requirement: Natural Science 10+, Scientific Specialist
(subject)

Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Knowledge)
within a subject where they are already a Scientific specialist. This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per subject.
Scientific Focus [2]
The person studies/studied within a scientific subject.
Requirement: Natural Science 4+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Knowledge)
within a subject they master. Choose one of the following subjects: Acoustics, Architecture, Astronomy, Atomic Physics,
Ballistics, Biology, Botany, Physics, Geology, Chemistry, Mathematics,
Material Science, Metallurgy, Meteorology, Optics, Zoology. Write down which subject the person masters. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per subject.
Scientific Specialist [2]
The person is a specialist within a scientific subject.
Requirement: Natural Science 7+, Scientific Focus
(subject)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Knowledge)
within a subject where they already have a scientific focus. This bonus is added to the previous bonus. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per subject.
**Close Combat**
Amateur Boxing [2]
The person knows the basics of boxing.
Requirement: Close Combat 4+
Rule: Increase the person's Defense (for close combat attacks) and
Endurance by one (+1) each.
Blocking [2]
The person is skilled at defending themselves in close combat.
Requirement: Close Combat 10+, Defensive Techniques
Rule: Increase the person's Defense (for close combat attacks) by two (+2).
Wrestling [2]
The person possesses knowledge in wrestling and grappling.
Requirement: Close Combat 7+, Brawling
Rule: Increase the person's Defense (for close combat attacks) by one (+1). The person gains a bonus (+5) when they attempt to grapple or disarm an opponent.
Defensive Techniques [3]
The person can utilize their martial arts defensively.
Requirement: Close Combat 7+, Martial Arts Training
Rule: Increase the person's Defense and Defense for close combat attacks by one (+1).
Draw and Attack [2]
The person can draw and attack in one motion with a close combat weapon.
Requirement: Close Combat 10+, Weapon Master
Rule: If the person succeeds in drawing a close combat weapon they are a weapon master with, they immediately perform the Initiative action Fight in the same Initiative, with the requirement that the drawn weapon is used. This attack receives a penalty (−5)
and lowers the person's Initiative by five (−5) after the attack action, in addition to any initiative modifications that result from the Initiative action's outcome.
Martial Arts Training [2]
The person is trained to fight in a coordinated and studied manner.
Requirement: Close Combat 4+
Rule: The person gains a bonus (+1) on dice rolls (Close
Combat) when they fight unarmed. Increase the person's Defense
(for close combat attacks) by one (+1).
Knockout Blow [3]
The person has learned how to strike truly hard blows against opponents.
Requirement: Close Combat 10+, Professional Boxing
Rule: When damage for an unarmed close combat attack exceeds the victim's Endurance value, the victim of the attack is either knocked down or becomes completely dizzy. This means the victim's Initiative is lowered by as much as the result of the damage.
Counterstrike [3]
The person is trained in executing surprising counterattacks.
Requirement: Close Combat 7+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on the defensive action
Strike Back.
Neutralize [3]
The person is knowledgeable in the art of effectively preventing an enemy from acting offensively.
Requirement: Close Combat 13+, Defensive Techniques
Rule: Increase the person's Defense (for close combat attacks) by one (+1). The person gains a bonus (+10) when they attempt to grapple or disarm an opponent.
Close Combat Weapon [2]
The person is skilled at handling a type of close combat weapon.
Requirement: Close Combat 4+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+2) when they use a specific type of close combat weapon (for example Knives or
Clubs). Write down which weapon type the person handles.
The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per weapon type.
Offensive Techniques [3]
The person can utilize their martial arts offensively.
Requirement: Close Combat 7+, Martial Arts Training
Rule: The person gains a bonus (+1) on dice rolls (Close
Combat) as well as on damage when they fight unarmed.
Parrying [2]
The person is skilled at deflecting attacks when they wield a weapon they master.
Requirement: Close Combat 7+, Close Combat Weapon (weapon type)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+2) on the defensive action
Defend in close combat, when they use a close combat weapon they can handle. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per weapon type.

Precision Attacks [3]
The person knows how to strike an enemy to inflict maximum damage.
Requirement: Close Combat 13+, Strike Series
Rule: Increase the person's Defense (for close combat attacks) by one (+1). The person's unarmed attacks no longer count as stunning.
Professional Boxing [3]
The person is a competent boxer.
Requirement: Close Combat 7+, Amateur Boxing
Rule: Increase the person's Wound Threshold and Endurance by one
(+1) each. Add one (+1) to damage when the person fights unarmed.
Clean Strikes [3]
The person is an expert at handling a specific weapon type.
Requirement: Close Combat 10+, Weapon Master
Rule: The person deals 1D extra in damage with the close combat weapons they are a Weapon Master with.
Self-Defense [2]
The person sacrifices their offensive capacity to avoid being attacked.
Requirement: Close Combat 4+
Rule: The person can choose to give themselves a bonus to
Defend against a close combat attack. Before the defender rolls their resistance roll they can choose to lower their
Initiative by any number of steps, to a minimum of six. For each step Initiative is lowered, the resistance roll result increases by one (+1).
Strike Series [2]
The person has knowledge of especially effective techniques for repeated attacks.
Requirement: Close Combat 10+, Offensive Techniques
Rule: Add the person's damage progression once more to damage when the person fights unarmed.
Brawling [2]
The person can fight in close combat.
Requirement: Close Combat 4+
Rule: The person gains a bonus (+1) on dice rolls (Close
Combat) as well as on damage when they fight unarmed.
Slugger [2]
The person uses their strength and mass to overwhelm an enemy with strikes.
Requirement: Close Combat 7+, Brawling
Rule: When the person uses unarmed close combat, they add their damage progression once more to the damage value when they attack to damage. The fighter cannot use the defensive action Defend, but must use Chance Defense, Push Back or Strike
Back until their turn in the following round.
Combat Ready [2]
The person is used to fighting.
Requirement: Close Combat 4+
Rule: Increase the person's Awareness by one (+1).
Chokehold [4]
The person knows techniques for effective choking.
Requirement: Close Combat 7+
Rule: The person may add 1D to damage during choking.
Two Weapon Master [3]
The person is masterful at handling two weapons at the same time.
Requirement: Close Combat 13+, Two Weapon Practitioner
Rule: The person can use two one-handed close combat weapons they are a weapon master with simultaneously. The weapons'
damage value is combined on a hit and 1D is added.
The attack receives a penalty (−5).
Two Weapon Practitioner [3]
The person is skilled at handling two weapons at the same time.
Requirement: Close Combat 7+, Weapon Master
Rule: The person can use two one-handed close combat weapons they are a weapon master with simultaneously. The weapons'
damage value is combined on a hit. The attack receives a penalty
(−2).
Weapon Master [2]
The person is an expert at handling a specific weapon type.
Requirement: Close Combat 7+, Close Combat Weapon (weapon type)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+3) when they use a close combat weapon they can already handle. Write down which type the person is an expert with. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per weapon type.
Takeover [2]
The person is skilled at disarming the opponent to then use the weapon themselves.
Requirement: Close Combat 10+, Defensive Techniques
Rule: If the person succeeds in performing a disarm and has at least one hand free, they may choose to catch the weapon instead of making it unusable for the victim.
**Reactions**
Lightning Deflection [2]
The person reacts to danger in close combat in the right way.
Requirement: Reactions 7+
Rule: Increase the person's Defense against close combat attacks by one (+1).
Duck [2]
The person reacts to danger in the right way.
Requirement: Reactions 4+
Rule: Increase the person's Defense by one (+1).
Explosion Sense [2]
The person reacts quickly to explosions.
Requirement: Reactions 4+
Rule: The person may roll a difficulty roll (Reactions) when they perceive that an explosion is imminent, for example if they perceive a bomb or a hand grenade. If the roll succeeds, the person has thrown themselves half their

combat movement away from the detonation site, though a maximum of long range. This action doesn't count as an action, though the person is considered prone which lowers their Initiative to 1.

Swift Movement [2]
The person moves quickly in combat.
Requirements: Reactions 7+
Rule: Increase the person's Combat Movement by one (+1).

Quick-Footed [2]
The person moves quickly in combat.
Requirements: Reactions 4+
Rule: Increase the person's Combat Movement by one (+1).

Quick to React [2]
The person reacts quickly to danger.
Requirements: Reactions 4+
Rule: Increase the person's Awareness by one (+1).

Spinal Reflex [2]
The person reacts to danger from long range in the right way.
Requirements: Reactions 7+
Rule: Increase the person's Defense against ranged attacks by one (+1).

See Opportunities [3]
The person can exploit a situation to their advantage.
Requirements: Reactions 7+
Rule: If the person succeeds exceptionally at any Initiative action, their Initiative increases by five steps (+5), beyond the normal effect from the action's outcome.

Combat Impulses [2]
In combat, the person reacts quickly and correctly.
Requirements: Reactions 7+, Quick to React
Rule: Increase the person's Awareness by one (+1).

Combat Reflexes [3]
The person knows how to act in combat to survive.
Requirements: Reactions 10+, Combat Impulses
Rule: Increase the person's Awareness by one (+1).

Exploit the Situation [3]
The person is skilled at exploiting situations to their advantage.
Requirements: Reactions 10+, See Opportunities
Rule: If the person succeeds exceptionally at any Initiative action, their Initiative increases by five steps (+5), beyond the normal effect from the action's outcome. This bonus is added to the effect from See Opportunities.

### Resistance

Hardened [2]
The person is less affected by a certain form of negative influence.
Requirements: Resistance 4+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Resistance) to resist one of the following: Resist fear/horror, Face death/murder others, Endure degradation/abuse, Endure violence/war, Withstand pain/torture. Write down which resistance the person possesses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per type of influence.

Extremely Hardened [3]
The person is not affected at all by a certain form of negative influence.
Requirements: Resistance 10+, Very Hardened
Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Resistance) to resist something they are already very hardened against. This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per type of influence.

Very Hardened [2]
The person is not affected by a certain form of negative influence.
Requirements: Resistance 7+, Hardened
Rule: The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Resistance) to resist something they are already hardened against. This bonus is added to the previous bonus. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per type of influence.

Insensitive to Pain [4]
The person barely feels pain anymore.
Requirements: Resistance 13+
Rule: Increase the person's Stamina by one (+1).

Fearless [3]
As long as the person can see a way out of a difficult situation, their self-confidence is at its peak.
Requirements: Resistance 4+
Rule: The person gets a bonus (+2) in threatening or risky situations, though not in combat, when they lack freedom of action.

Steel Resolve [2]
The person can steel themselves against, and endure, pain better.
Requirements: Resistance 10+
Rule: Increase the person's Stamina by one (+1).

### Mobility

Fall Technique [2]
The person is trained in falling in ways to minimize damage.
Requirements: Mobility 7+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on difficulty rolls (Mobility) during falls & collisions, see page 267.

Fleet-Footed [2]
The person is incredibly fast.
Requirements: Mobility 10+, Fast
Rule: Increase the person's Combat Movement by one (+1).

Graceful [3]
The person is a specialist in a form of body control.
Requirements: Mobility 10+, Agile (area)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Mobility) within an area where they are already agile. This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per area.

Mobile [1]
The person is skilled in a form of body control.
Requirements: Mobility 4+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Mobility) within one of the following areas: Acrobatics/Balance/Gymnastics, Dance, Jump/Climb, Crawl/Slither, Modeling/Posing, Sneak, Escapology/Contortionist. Write down which area the person masters. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per area.

Agile [2]
The person is experienced in a form of body control.
Requirements: Mobility 7+, Mobile (area)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Mobility) within an area where they are already mobile. This bonus is added to the previous bonus. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per area.

Fast [2]
The person is fast.
Requirements: Mobility 7+
Rule: Increase the person's Combat Movement by one (+1).

### Mind

Aura of Calm [2]
The person radiates calm.
Requirements: Mind 4+
Rule: Increase the person's Impression by one (+1).

Balanced Psyche [4]
The person needs minimal sleep.
Requirements: Mind 10+, Self-Discipline
Rule: The person becomes rested in a short time and only needs to sleep four hours per day.

Inner Peace [3]
The person has an inner peace that gives spiritual serenity.
Requirements: Mind 10+, Strong-Willed
Rule: Increase the person's Will to Live by one (+1).

Inner Reserve [2]
The person can mobilize their energies when it really counts.
Requirements: Mind 7+, Self-Discipline
Rule: The person can once per game session directly restore Disorientation by drawing energy from their inner reserves. If they succeed at a difficulty roll (Mind), they immediately recover as much Disorientation as the value on the highest die.

Ice Cold [3]
The person is completely emotionless when killing defenseless people.
Requirements: Mind 4+
Rule: The person gets a bonus (+5) when executing defenseless people.

Come to One's Senses [2]
The person's consciousness allows them to quickly come to their senses again after being knocked out.

the result of the value on all dice. The person needs about a minute to perform the action. When the person wants to wake up, it takes about a minute and requires a successful roll (Mind). Each attempt gives the person as much Disorientation as the result of all dice in the roll. The person is perceived by non-medically trained people as deceased. A successful medical examination (Medicine) with a penalty (−5) is required to discover that the person is actually alive.

### Create

Outstanding Creator [2]
The person is experienced in an artistic area.
Requirements: Create 7+, Creator (area)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+3) on dice rolls (Create) within an area where they are already a creator. This bonus is added to the previous bonus. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per area.

Creator [2]
The person is skilled in an artistic area.
Requirements: Create 4+
Rule: The person has a bonus (+2) on dice rolls (Create) within one of the following creative areas: Photography, Forge artwork/sculptures, Forge documents/papers/licenses, Forge money, Forge jewelry, Disguise/Makeup, Compose music/songs, Cooking, Paint/Draw, Poetry/Poems, Write journalistic texts, Write fiction texts, Sculpt. Write down which area the person masters. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per area.

Virtuoso [3]
The person is a specialist in an artistic area.
Requirements: Create 10+, Outstanding Creator (area)
Rule: The person has a bonus (+5) on dice rolls (Create) within an area where they are already an outstanding creator. This bonus is added to other bonuses. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per area.

### Firearms

Executioner [4]
The person is trained in efficiently murdering people.
Requirements: Firearms 13+, Weapon Expert
Rule: Every time a target suffers at least one Wound, an additional Wound is inflicted. This expertise can only be performed with firearms the person is a weapon expert with.

Controlled Fire [3]
The person fires fewer rounds during repeated fire.
Requirements: Firearms 10+, Weapon Expert
Rule: If the number of fired rounds is Sum of the result on all dice x2, it is lowered to Sum of the result on all dice, Sum of the result on all dice is lowered to Result on the highest die, and Result on the highest die is lowered to Result on the lowest die. Those with Result on the lowest die lower the number of fired rounds to one round. This expertise can only be performed with firearms on which the person is a weapon expert.

Requirements: Mind 10+, Self-Discipline
Rule: The person gets a bonus (+2) on rolls (Stamina) to regain consciousness (see page 263).

Power Source [3]
The person can draw help from their inner forces.
Requirements: Mind 10+, Inner Reserve
Rule: The person can once per game session directly restore Disorientation by drawing power from their inner reserve. If the person succeeds at a difficulty roll (Mind), they immediately recover as much Disorientation as the result of the value on all dice.

Cheat Death [5]
The person can rise from the dead.
Requirements: Mind 13+, Self-Discipline
Rule: When the person dies, they may roll a difficulty roll (Will to Live) with a penalty (−10) once per minute, for as many minutes as the value on the lowest die in the failed difficulty roll (Will to Live). If any of these rolls succeed, the person lives again, but retains all injuries they had at the moment of death — though they are not mortally wounded. Should any of the rolls fail exceptionally, the person is irrevocably dead. This expertise may only be used once per game session.

Self-Discipline [3]
The person has mental methods for achieving calm.
Requirements: Mind 4+
Rule: The person can improve their Well-Being by one level with a successful difficulty roll (Mind). Using the expertise takes about ten minutes, and during this time the person must be undisturbed and concentrated. The expertise may be used once per game session.

Self-Healing [5]
By redirecting the body's resources, the person can heal injuries on themselves.
Requirements: Mind 13+, Self-Discipline
Rule: The person can recover Wounds once per game session by rolling a difficulty roll (Mind). For the person to roll, they need an hour of seclusion for concentration and energy focusing. The outcome is read below.

Exceptionally successful: The person heals as many Wounds as the value on the lowest die.
Successful: The person heals one Wound.
Failed: The person heals no Wounds.
Exceptionally failed: The person takes one Wound from the exertion and must immediately roll (Will to Live).

Strong-Willed [3]
The person has a strong will to live.
Requirements: Mind 7+
Rule: Increase the person's Will to Live by one (+1).

Play Dead [4]
The person can control all their bodily functions.
Requirements: Mind 10+, Self-Discipline
Rule: If the person succeeds at a difficulty roll (Mind), they can play dead for up to as many hours as

Suppressive Fire [3]
The character is trained in effectively returning fire in difficult situations.
Requirements: Firearms 7+, Weapon Expert
Rule: When the character uses the defensive action Return Fire with a weapon they are a weapon expert with, the penalty is only Recoil x 2 instead of x 3 (see page 260).

Lead in the Air [2]
The character doesn't spare bullets when firing automatic weapons.
Requirements: Firearms 4+, Weapon Handling (weapon category)
Rule: The character can choose to gain a bonus (+1) to hit and damage when using an automatic weapon they can handle. In return, the number of rounds expended is doubled.

Multiple Targets [4]
The character is an expert at shooting at many different targets with a weapon in each hand.
Requirements: Firearms 13+, Several Targets
Rule: When the character uses one of the Initiative Actions Shoot with Two Weapons or Shoot Automatic Fire with Two Weapons, with a weapon they are a weapon expert with, they can choose to shoot at as many targets as the value of the lowest die. The shooter only rolls once. All targets get to defend themselves, and if anyone is damaged, the weapons' combined damage value is counted, without further modification.

Suppressing Fire [2]
The character focuses on trying to pin down enemies by continuously shooting at their general position.
Requirements: Firearms 4+, Weapon Handling (weapon category)
Rule: The character can choose to take a penalty (−2) to hit and damage when using the initiative action Sweep with Automatic Weapon with an automatic weapon they can handle. The number of rounds expended is doubled. In return, those being shot at reduce their Initiative by five steps (−5).

"Close Doesn't Count!" [3]
The character can use all their combat knowledge to best avoid gunfire.
Requirements: Firearms 10+, "Take Cover!"
Rule: Increase the character's Defense (against ranged attacks) by two (+2).

Marksmanship [3]
The character is an expert at handling rifles with scopes.
Requirements: Firearms 7+, Weapon Expert (rifle with scope)
Rule: The character increases their weapon's damage value by 1D when using the Initiative Action Sniper Shot and shooting with a weapon they are a weapon expert with.

Shot Placement [2]
The character is skilled at hitting non-vital parts of the target with firearms.
Requirements: Firearms 7+, Weapon Expert
Rule: Attacks made with the combat action Shoot Aimed Fire and with a firearm the character is a weapon expert with can, if the shooter wishes, be counted as stunning instead of lethal.

Double Hit [3]
The character can shoot at two separate targets with one weapon in a single motion.
Requirements: Firearms 10+, Weapon Expert (any one-handed firearm)
Rule: When the character uses the Initiative Action Shoot Snap Shot, with a weapon they are a weapon expert with, they can choose to shoot at two targets. The shooter only rolls once. Both targets get to defend themselves, and if anyone is damaged, the weapon's normal damage value is counted, without modification.

Dual Reload [2]
The character is an expert at reloading two one-handed weapons simultaneously.
Requirements: Firearms 10+, Two-Handed Shooting
Rule: When the character uses the Initiative Action Reload, they can attempt to reload two one-handed weapons they are a weapon expert with simultaneously. This gives a penalty (−5) to the dice roll.

Several Targets [3]
The character is an expert at shooting at two different targets, with a weapon in each hand.
Requirements: Firearms 10+, Two-Handed Shooting
Rule: When the character uses one of the Initiative Actions Shoot with Two Weapons or Shoot Automatic Fire with Two Weapons, with a weapon they are a weapon expert with, they can choose to shoot at two targets. The shooter only rolls once. Both targets get to defend themselves, and if anyone is damaged, the weapons' combined damage value is counted, without further modification.

Critical Hits [2]
The character is skilled at hitting critical points on the target with firearms.
Requirements: Firearms 10+, Shot Placement
Rule: The character increases the damage value by two (+2) when using a firearm they are a weapon expert with.

Bullet Storm [2]
The character likes to fire a lot of automatic fire.
Requirements: Firearms 7+, Lead in the Air
Rule: When the character performs an Initiative Action with an automatic weapon that fires multiple rounds, they can choose before the roll to double the ammunition expenditure to give themselves a bonus (+2) to both hit and damage value. The disadvantage is that they may not use the defensive action Defend, but must use Chance Defense, Return Fire, or Strike Back until their turn in the next round. This expertise can only be performed with firearms the character is a weapon expert with.

Massive Counter-Fire [3]
The character is trained in giving effective return fire with two weapons in difficult situations.
Requirements: Firearms 7+, Two-Handed Shooting
Rule: When the character uses the defensive action Return Fire, with two one-handed weapons they are a weapon expert with, the weapon's damage increases by 1D per 2D in damage value (minimum +1D).

Quick Draw [2]
The character can draw and shoot in a single motion.
Requirements: Firearms 10+, Weapon Expert
Rule: If the character succeeds in drawing one or more firearms they are a weapon expert with, they immediately fire the drawn weapon at the same Initiative. This attack takes a penalty (−5) and lowers the character's Initiative by five (−5) after the attack action, beyond any initiative modifications that come from the Initiative Action's outcome.

Heavy Weapons Specialist [4]
The character is an expert at handling heavy weapons.
Requirements: Firearms 7+, Weapon Expert (heavy weapon)
Rule: The weapon's damage value increases by 1D when the character uses a heavy weapon they are a weapon expert with.

Battle-Hardened [2]
The character is used to using firearms in combat.
Requirements: Firearms 4+
Rule: Increase the character's Awareness by one (+1).

Take Cover [2]
The character sacrifices offensive capacity to avoid being hit.
Requirements: Firearms 4+
Rule: The character can choose to give themselves a bonus to Defend against a ranged attack. Before the defender rolls their resistance roll, they lower their Initiative by any number of steps, but at least to six. For each step the Initiative is lowered, a (+1) bonus is added to the resistance roll's result.

"Take Cover!" [2]
The character can use their combat knowledge to more effectively avoid being hit.
Requirements: Firearms 7+
Rule: Increase the character's Defense (against ranged attacks) by one (+1).

Two-Handed Shooting [3]
The character is an expert at shooting with a weapon in each hand.
Requirements: Firearms 7+, Weapon Expert (one-handed firearm)
Rule: The character has a bonus (+2) on the Initiative Actions Shoot with Two Weapons and Shoot Automatic Fire with Two Weapons with weapons they are a weapon expert with.

"Two in the Head and One in the Chest" [4]
The character is trained in assassin techniques.
Requirements: Firearms 13+, Weapon Expert
Rule: The character can use the Initiative Action Shoot Snap Shot in an alternative way against a single target at most three meters away. The shooter must have higher Initiative than the target. If the shooter succeeds, an additional 2D is added to the damage value. Furthermore, exactly three rounds are always crossed off. This expertise can only be performed with firearms the character is a weapon expert with.

Weapon Expert [1]
The character is an expert at handling a specific weapon model.
Requirements: Firearms 4+, Weapon Handling (weapon category)
Rule: The character has a bonus (+3) when using a specific weapon model (for example, Calder M2245 or Rais H100) they can already handle. Write down which weapon model the character is an expert with. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per weapon model. The different models are found on pages 106–117. Note that the bonus applies to all combat actions where the weapon is an active part—from drawing the weapon to shooting and reloading.

Weapon Handling [2]
The character is trained in handling a category of firearms.
Requirements: Firearms 4+
Rule: The character has a bonus (+2) when using a firearm from one of the following categories: Automatic Pistol, Rifle, Shotgun, Submachine Gun, Machine Gun, Revolver, Heavy Weapons. Write down which category the character can handle. The expertise can be chosen multiple times, but only once per weapon category. The different categories correspond to those on pages 106–117. Note that the bonus applies to all combat actions where the weapon is an active part—from drawing the weapon to shooting and reloading.

Gun Play [2]
Requirements: Firearms 10+, Weapon Expert
Rule: If the character incapacitates an enemy with a firearm they are a weapon expert with, their Initiative increases by two (+2).

Evasion [2]
The character is skilled at avoiding damage, regardless of situation.
Requirements: Firearms 10+, "Take Cover!"
Rule: Increase the character's Defense by one (+1).

### Deduction

Genius [3]
The character is a specialist at drawing conclusions from clues.
Requirements: Deduction 10+, Smart (area)
