The family also has a large fortune from the time when they had a monopoly on textile trade from the east.
Today information is the family's commodity, as is the spreading of disinformation. Kuinn is also a well-known cigar brand.
**Palisar**
Palisar is one of the smaller families that earns a large portion of its money from pornography, erotic literature and clothing.
Above all men at the front read the Palisar family's richly illustrated books and at seedy porn theaters in the cities one perversity after another is shown in the family's name.
The members of the family are known for decadence and erotic escapades and both the men and women are known for being skilled in the art of seduction.
Palisar is also a well-known fashion name and besides tailored overcoats and suits they have a large selection of underwear of an erotic nature.
**Revenir**
Outwardly the Revenir family engages in real estate business and trade but it is an open secret that large sums of money come in from illegal business in the underworld. The family controls large parts of Sandukar's drug trade and has influence over prostitution on the streets.
The Revenir family is somewhat ill-regarded within its own clan. The only real allies the family has is Palisar. Few dare to cross Revenir who thanks to contacts, information and influence can strike hard and brutally against its opponents.
**Kriger**
The machinery of war is driven by human blood, will and fury. Sacrifices must be made in the struggle to preserve the Empire.
Soldiers must suffer and die to maintain the balance.
The General from clan Kriger regards the map that lies before him. His gaze sweeps over the northern front — trenches, ruined cities, bunkers and fortifications.
A hell of mud and rusty barbed wire. He looks up and puts his monocle in the uniform's breast pocket.
The adjutants and advisors stand at strict attention along the walls. The General feels their fear and despises it. With a cold voice he gives orders to advance on all battle lines. The orders are noted down and then telegraphed out to the hungry and tired men in the trenches.
The General then orders his car to be brought forth.
He wants to be present himself to observe the slaughter.
Clan Kriger has primary responsibility for the Empire's armies. It is an important part of the State and stands close to clan
Arkady, but more as subordinate than as equal.
The clan is relatively large and many of the members sit in high positions in the army and at the Ministry of War. There are also plenty of engineers, inventors and technological innovators within the clan who construct machines of destruction and weapons.
The clan has influence in the cities but one finds many of the members at the fronts and at forts out in the wastelands.
They are disciplined and tyrannical and within the clan a draconian and swift justice system is applied. When discipline falters the members often fall prey to senseless rage and an unquenchable bloodthirst. Not infrequently the younger officers within the clan personally lead the soldiers across the battlefield.
The clan's administrative headquarters consists of a black concrete skyscraper in the center of Sandukar, decorated with symbols and statues of influential clan members. To the skyscraper, called the Horn, all information is sent and here are also the most skilled tacticians as well as the clan's head
Archduke Sebastien van Gralen-Kriger.
The clan has little control in society except in the military domain. The members do however have some social status as they are seen as brave heroes fighting for the Empire's preservation. The clan has very few internal conflicts but is somewhat rigid as members seldom do anything that doesn't follow all laws and regulations to the letter. Few want to antagonize them even though quite a bit of intrigue happens behind their backs.
Kriger's members usually wear strict suits, dresses or uniforms in everyday life, their straight posture, well-trained bodies and somewhat stiff manner usually reveal which clan they belong to. Decorations and medals are displayed with pride. They often carry concealed weapons to whatever event it may be.
**Brana**
In the northern Arkady mountains lies the Brana family's golden calf. The family owns several large mining companies and earns ample sums of money from the valuable metals they mine.
In the cities the family has begun competing with clan Zovrin and challenging the banking system. Its riches are used to erect monuments and raise statues in memory of great battles and significant ancestors.
Unlike most families within clan Kriger where women have a hidden role, the Brana family has several high-ranking women in its ranks who all hold significant positions in society.

### Gault

The notorious Gault family earns large sums of money from its labor camps where undesirable people toil without either pay or rights. The family also has several mining enterprises and is known for terrible deals and miserable conditions.
The family has great influence in Vadaja but relatively little presence in Sandukar.
People within the Gault family are known for being sadistic, racist and in many ways totally emotionally cold toward the suffering of others.
**Gralen**
The backbone of the Empire's army is in many ways made up of the Gralen family and its military leaders. The family has great influence over the military and most training camps where soldiers receive their basic training are under its control.
The family has also managed to earn money from the wars and the equipment they require. Gralen owns factories where seamstresses sit in rows sewing uniforms, manufacturing canteens, tents, tarpaulins and other things needed in the field.
The Gralen family has become a beast that feeds itself. The members of the family are very proud of their history and have great knowledge of their ancestors' exploits.
**Hauser**
The Empire's largest weapons manufacturer is the Hauser family, which manufactures everything from automatic pistols to fragmentation bombs, artillery units and mines. Family members have an almost loving attitude toward their firearms and it is said that hell closes the day one encounters a member of the Hauser family who willingly goes unarmed.
Hauser owns several factories around the Empire but has the greatest influence in Vadaja and Sandukar. Recently the family has entered the film industry and produced several violent action films where their weapons are extensively used and where the state flag is held high.
**Kaldberg**
The Kaldberg family has a long and bloody history as warlords and ravagers. They still have cavalry units under their control and rely on old methods with lightning strikes, guerrilla warfare and sabotage to achieve their goals.
The family is unreliable and has several times betrayed agreements and regulations to instead grab riches and avenge old wrongs. It has gained increased influence in the army recently and handles much of the logistics along the northern railways.
**Rötz**
The Rötz family is often called the steel kings of the north. Even though the family doesn't have such great influence over the mining itself, it is the one that smelts the metal and transports it across the empire. Rötz has several large factories that produce everything from nuts to entire ships. Recently the family's engineers have begun working to develop tanks for the army that will change the static trench wars.
For being of clan Kriger, Rötz is a relatively social family whose members can often be seen in various contexts in Sandukar.

Beloved Max,
I have painful news. As you suspected I have earlier in my life, before we met and you opened my eyes, done things I am not proud of, things that I
knew would come back later. Now it has happened. I feel close to you now, now that I open myself to you. A vile man who ensnared me, beat me and humiliated me, a man I fled from, has found me. He will kill me as he is not a man who takes lightly the fact that I escaped his wrath. But we have a chance! I managed to lure him to the other end of the city so we have the chance to flee together. We can take your car and the cash you said you had at home in the safe? We can build a future together, somewhere else, just the two of us. Come to the harbor, to our place. I long for you and need you, more than ever! Yours forever
—Talia

### Turga

The dreaded Turga family has its fingers in many affairs. Unlike most other families within the Kriger line, they own no large mining operations or factories. Instead, they have focused on administration and state information management.
Turga is clearly the family within the line that has the greatest insight into the state bureaucracy, and its members hold positions as bureaucrats and politicians, high-ranking military officers and police. They maintain discipline among soldiers and search for dissidents. They often keep to the cities, where they have power over police and private security firms, or to the fronts where they research psychological warfare and conduct interrogations of prisoners.
The family is known for having close contact with the
Arkady line, and it is said that many state commissars have their origin among the members, something representatives from the Ministry of Truth nervously laugh at.
**Mengal**
The woman on the steel gurney screams as the narrow drill penetrates her temple. The scream turns into a gurgle and the woman's gaze becomes empty. Instruments are inserted. The operation proceeds quickly. Part of the brain is cut apart. A syringe injects chemicals. Then the skin flaps are sewn together. The man from the Mengal line smiles contentedly. The lobotomies are becoming ever more effective. The lifers owned by the line can now be programmed to become obedient workers, pleasure slaves, and loyal soldiers who lack fear. The man walks with his escort away from the tiled laboratory and its antiseptic air. He stops by the tall windows and gazes out over the factory city—his domains. Against the vault of heaven stretch hundreds of chimneys spewing out their sooty smoke. Soot flakes drift down over the workers' quarters like black snow. Never has he seen anything so beautiful.
Factories and labor camps are the Mengal line's domains. Under the line members' supervision, the wilderness's raw materials are transformed into architectural wonders and all manner of utilitarian objects. On vast rail yards, locomotives and train cars are built, in air hangars zeppelins are constructed, and on assembly lines motor vehicles are manufactured. The line members themselves sit leaning back and decide what shall be built.
The line has several subfamilies, but most of the workers are lifers who wear themselves out without either wages or rights.
Within the line there are plenty of doctors and psychologists who have studied the human body and the human psyche and developed several methods for lobotomy and mind control that make the workers become will-less and harmless.
The line is spread across all cities but has its headquarters in Sandukar, in the first sector of the Machine Cities.
Here they have a seat of concrete, steel, and cogwheels where administration is conducted. The seat houses tens of thousands of workplaces and is ironically called Paradise in common parlance.
Mengal's head, Archiatrist Nadya van Arghezi, oversees all the line's undertakings and is a feared woman in the Empire, as her opponents have a tendency to disappear so without a trace that not even the overarchivists can find documentation.
Recently the line has begun using the workers to create will-less soldiers, which is viewed with great concern primarily by the Kriger line, as one cannot know how capable the soldiers are and what the line might think to use them for.
Members of the Mengal line encountered at social events have a tendency to be pale and thin. They wear fashionable clothes but often lack the social charm and elegance that most other lines possess.
**Alexos**
The Alexos family has a connection to the Janoistic part of the church, even though many members have openly renounced religion.
Instead, the family has tried to focus on administration and education—its members hold high positions at most major universities, medical schools, and libraries.
The family rests on very old knowledge, even such as is officially forbidden, and uses this knowledge to bargain politically with other families and lines.
**Arghezi**
The family within the Mengal line that has the most contact with members of other lines is without question the Arghezi family. The members often operate in public and function as diplomats and advisors and own several large industries.
The Arghezi family devotes much time to trade and buys raw materials to then sell finished products. Part of the family manages a large portion of Sandukar's meat production and owns a large number of livestock farms and slaughterhouses.
**Eviza**
The Eviza family has achieved great success in research in medicine and pharmaceuticals. The members study human organs and how to best strengthen and destroy them, for as much as they are researchers in life, they are pioneers in death.
Members of the Eviza family have great knowledge of how to use chemical gases as weapons, and at the fronts they conduct their hideous experiments. They also research how to alter humans through mutation and breeding to create a super race. Eviza's institutions in Sandukar are always well-guarded.
**Harlos**
Insular, wealthy, and utterly fearless are designations that fit well the members of the Harlos family. They have carried out several bold projects and are now leading in the development of flying machines for the army and for private use. They invest large sums in constructing more efficient flying machines and more precise weaponry and hope that air dominance will one day become as important as the infantry strength corps' conquests.
The family has also earned some money from the car and motorcycle industry, particularly when it comes to the robust products sold to the army.
**Luri**
The well-off Luri family is known for its architectural creations and vast mechanical constructions.
When it comes to dams, coal power plants, mining equipment, and the science of durability and construction, the family is world-leading.

Luri owns large amounts of real estate, and many of the members are architects and engineers. Recently they have bought up several radio channels and newspapers, which has given them an influence over media that has in turn driven them into conflict with the Guiskard line.
**Mugrona**
The dreaded Mugrona family has for a long time concentrated on the human psyche and the best way to keep it enslaved. The members control several companies that in turn have control over factories and labor camps where the lifer workers get to function as experimental subjects.
Many within the family are skilled in medicine and psychology and use lobotomy, chemicals, and hypnosis to create efficient and will-less servants. Recently great progress has been made in directing the servants toward different tasks in order to thus create capable workers, passionate pleasure slaves, and will-less soldiers.
**Verdos**
The Verdos family is known throughout the Empire for its vast zeppelins. The family was the first to take to the skies with its airships and has maintained dominance over air traffic since then.
In secret, the members work against the Harlos family while simultaneously researching in secret their own types of flying machines and more modern zeppelins that shall be able to travel faster and more safely over great distances.
The Verdos family has control over a large number of fortresses and research institutes located on hard-to-reach mountaintops and isolated on islands.
**Orlok**
The forests, mountains, fields, and lakes are all resources that are felled, blasted, and consumed in the hunt for raw materials. Modern mechanical machines break brown coal out of the ground while drills and explosives tear holes in the mountains' roots.
Timber from devastated forest areas drives blast furnaces and vast refineries spew out chemicals and waste products into the rivers. Nature is mutilated, reshaped, and plundered of its resources. Along the railways the raw material is sent to the cities where it is processed into food, machines, and weapons.
The man from the Orlok line and his kind are the wilderness's pioneers. They find new resources and assets to feed the hungry beast that the State has created.
Unlike the other lines, Orlok has its power in the wilderness. Here they erect their industries to secure nature's raw materials. They have multitudes of subfamilies that all struggle to surpass each other. These in turn have multitudes of workers beneath them, who are paid pittances for dangerous work.
Members of the Orlok line despise the other lines'
sedentary life in the cities, as true freedom exists in the wilderness.
The line members erect provisional labor camps where resources can be found, and there workers extract around the clock nature's assets. Additionally, they are landowners and have the task of overseeing agriculture and livestock management.
Since the Todescu line broke free, Orlok has also taken over a large part of control over the railways and logistics within the realm, but as yet everything is far from running smoothly.
The line's political seat is a stately self-sufficient estate, nicknamed the Farm, with a magnificent inner courtyard near Sandukar's center in the Spiran district. Here lives the line's head, the aged Grand Duke Karel d'Petkovich. He carefully studies the reports he receives from his subordinates and plans new expeditions to places where refineries shall be built. Members of the Orlok line are known for not being particularly meticulous or patient, which often causes them trouble and leads to many workers' deaths.
Outside the cities, line members wear practical clothes, but in social situations they usually change into lavishly decorated uniforms. The men then often wear traditional swords at their sides.
**Jasonek**
Explorers and pioneers are plentiful within the Jasonek family, and through history they have had several famous explorers in their ranks. The family devotes much time to finding new assets and natural resources, information that they then sell to other families within the line—or even to other lines.
The Jasonek family is active in many areas but has no total dominance anywhere. Its largest income comes from the timber industry. Family members are those who without question have the greatest insight into the Empire's geography, and it is also said that they rest on secret knowledge of hidden and mysterious places.
**Kostash**
Black is the color that members of the Kostash family have on their suits, dresses, and coats—black is the smoke that billows out from their coal power plants, and blackened is the ground in the areas they control.
Kostash has great riches for being such a small family and owns several railway companies and manages railway maintenance.
Family members are known for being sickly and weak but possessing great intellect. They are disliked and feared as they often indulge in assassinations—preferably of all through poisoning. Even within the family, premature deaths are many.
**Lachek**
Lachek—the largest family within the Orlok line is spread across the entire Empire and controls large parts of food production. Everything from cultivation of beets and fruit to the meat market lies under its control. Recently the family has gotten increasingly more competition in the market and uses dishonest methods to maintain its dominance.
Despite the Lachek family being large and relatively wealthy, it is ill-regarded and the members are seen as easily influenced and somewhat stupid. There are clear signs of inbreeding in the family, and hereditary diseases and madness are very common.
"Anger from lies is eternal. Anger from truth is transient."
— Legate Felix van Kordar de Zovrin

### Navod

The members of the Navod family usually keep to the cities where they manage administration and negotiate trade agreements with other clans and families. They own factories and refineries and strive to gain greater influence in the state bureaucracy.

Despite their important diplomatic position, many of the family members are boorish and clumsy in social contexts and known for their gluttony in food and drink, which has caused many of them to suffer from severe obesity. They also have an ability to treat most others as their subjects, which is hardly appreciated.

### Petkovich

Day and night, the Petkovich family's oil pumps drain the earth's interior of black gold. Entire fields can be filled with oil pumps and from tall steel towers equipped with searchlights, the treasure is guarded greedily.

The family has many competitors and has been forced to devote large resources to soldiers who protect its properties—sabotage occurs daily, however, and in some places the oil wells are on fire.

The family members are known to be very spoiled and arrogant and have openly expressed their contempt for the other clans. Always wearing a golden flower on their person is important to them.

### Yakutsk

Along the rivers of the Empire travel massive diesel-powered steel ships bearing the Yakutsk family symbol. If the other families in the Orlok clan handle the extraction of raw materials, Yakutsk transports them to their destinations.

The family is known for being able to keep quiet about most business deals if the payment is good enough—they can also move things through inspections and customs without great risk. Rumor has it that family members engage in plunder and sometimes have competitors' ships attacked and sunk, but no evidence of this can be found.

### Zakov

The Zakov family has large work camps throughout the Empire and has focused on extracting ore deposits. Members of the family are known to be honest and understanding but have shown several times that they don't hesitate to crush those who deceive or go against them.

Recently, the family has been working to establish grand forts and defensive works blasted into the mountains for the army and for their own use.

### Todescu

Their seat is destroyed. Their name is tainted. The State has turned its will against them and fights to annihilate them. But now they are free and now they have the opportunity to influence their future. No longer shall they be bullied by the other clans—no more envy, no more humiliation. Now they are feared and have taken up the fight to raise their own honor. The fire of faith burns within them all and the fire of hatred burns their enemies to ash. They are no longer alone, the fallen angel stands at their side—like them he was bullied and persecuted and condemned to wander the earth for eternity. These thoughts swirl through the mind of the man of clan Todescu after he finished his prayer in the trench. He grips his rifle with dirty fingers and raises it with a roar toward the blood-red vault of heaven—the time of vengeance has come.

Todescu is no longer part of the State and the Empire. The clan, which has always been one of the least influential, was previously responsible for the logistics and construction of the great railways outside the realm. Its members were always regarded with suspicion because most were of the Hraghic people and were strictly religious. The clan therefore had weak power in Sandukar and the majority of members lived in the eastern province of Presvedonia.

The oppression against Todescu and its new, influential leaders who burned for the religious faith caused members to rebel against the State. Everything happened in the shadows at first. The steel mills in the eastern province began secretly manufacturing weapons and war materiel and the people were filled with religious ecstasy and conviction. The war has now raged for some time and the clan is condemned—only in the breakaway province can it act openly.

Todescu's formerly magnificent seat in Sandukar is now a burnt-out ruin left as a warning of what happens to traitors. The clan still has spies and sympathizers in the Empire, however, who secretly help them—these individuals live a very dangerous life.

The clan's members usually wear their hair long according to Hraghic culture. A number of them are now within the army and church where they have important roles as leaders and warriors.

### Ferescu

Driving the machinery of war is very costly in terms of natural resources and the Ferescu family does its best to produce what is needed for the soldiers to continue fighting against the State's lurking troops.

Ferescu has several important mining areas under its control and has also begun extracting oil on an increasingly large scale. The family is relatively bullied by the other families and constantly pressed from all sides to produce more materiel.

### Invida

The Invida family was previously responsible for work on the railways in the Empire and now handles communications in Presvedonia. Under the family's supervision, railway tracks are laid through forests and over mountain ridges in the realm and family members ensure there are transports for troops and materiel.

Invida is the family that has the most contacts and spies outside Presvedonia.

### Mâcelar

Mâcelar has a history of war behind it. The family has led its people in countless battles against the enemy in the north and now uses this knowledge to liberate its realm. Many of the family members have experience from the horrors of battlefields and participate in the fighting themselves. They focus primarily on the State's forces in the west and let the Rasbotny family and its troops fight against the northern front in Sadovia.

The family members are filled with religious hysteria and hubris which makes them somewhat reckless. In battle they gladly use axes during assaults.

### Nălucă

Previously Nălucă was a vassal family to clan Arkady and is still suspected of serving it in one way or another. Many family members have been imprisoned because of this but no evidence could be presented and since the family has participated in the freedom struggle with at least as much intensity as other clan families, they have begun to be more accepted.

The family has several large assets when it comes to arable land and livestock and is now working hard to expand its production.

### Rasbotny

The Rasbotny family has a past as warriors and now commands many parts of the fighting units together with the Mâcelar family. Members have focused on the fighting in the north and against Sadovia and in the shipyards they oversee the construction of a fleet that will be able to challenge the State's destroyers.

Within the family it is very common to move with the help of horses and the family has introduced many mounted units that can quickly move through forests and rugged terrain where state motor vehicles cannot reach.

### Sânge

Those who stand closest to the Fallen One and interpret his commands are the Sânge family. The family functions as the Fallen One's black priesthood and its servants search for infidels and rebels in the realm. Sânge has become an inquisition that captures, interrogates and tortures those who oppose the new regime. It is perceived that the women within the family are much crueler than the men, especially when it comes to torturing, or extracting information from, the State's supporters.

### Vrajba

Vrajba is one of the smaller families that was previously excluded from clan Todescu but has now returned with renewed power. Madness seems to characterize the members in one way or another and they are also filled with a hatred toward their former oppressors in Sadovia and the entire state structure.

Vrajba has gained great control over the cities and has founded a new bureaucracy where the people shall have the right to choose.

Members of the family handle information dissemination in a very active way, they spread leaflets with demoralizing truths about the Empire over the State's trenches, among other things.

### Zovrin

From the office window one can see out over the eternal city. A myriad of lights from millions of inhabitants trying to keep the night and cold away. In the alleys, street urchins warm themselves by small fires, in the apartments families gather around radios, in the manors the rich eat silent dinners.

All are enslaved, all realize the power of money and cannot resist its temptation. Money can pervert the strongest mind, turn brother against brother and force people into submission. The very aura of wealth can strike people with speechlessness and awaken admiration and envy. This insight caresses the mind of the woman from clan Zovrin as she sits at her desk at night and seals the fate of yet another citizen with a simple signature.

The members of clan Zovrin are administrators and bureaucrats whose main purpose is to watch over Sandukar's economy, banking and trade. The clan has even given its name to the Empire's currency. Within clan Zovrin most members are very rich and thus powerful.

The members are not particularly numerous, Zovrin is the second smallest clan after Arkady, but with money one can buy both followers and loyal servants. Thus the clan is one of the most powerful in the Empire. It has, however, only important contacts in the upper parts of society; dealing with the poor other than by granting them loans with outrageous interest rates is rarely economically defensible.

The clan's head, legate Felix van Kordar de Zovrin, is a stately, middle-aged man with a calculating mind. He lives at the very top of the clan's stately round skyscraper—a mighty construction of pillars, bridges, black steel, stone and glass called the Midas Tower, located in the central parts of the Mint. The tower houses endless filing cabinets, filled vaults and financial reports. Only trusted ones may tread the skyscraper's polished marble floors.

Clan Zovrin leads the persecution of the Hraghic people. This largely as revenge for the enormous sums of money the clan lost when Todescu broke free from the realm without repaying its debts.

Members of clan Zovrin are usually conservatively well-dressed in discreet creations and indulge in expensive pleasures. Most have blond hair in strict hairstyles. Money is their passion and many of them indulge in gambling and games of chance.

### Dunarmco

The Dunarmco family consists of arrogant gamblers and libertines who spend much time on riches and pleasures. Their wealth comes largely from the mines in the former homeland of Kovan but almost all members of the family live in Sandukar and let the mines be managed by various companies.

There is an exclusive watch brand that bears the family name but the watches are only sold within clan Zovrin.

### Egato

The Egato family deals largely with foodstuffs of various kinds but also owns many business chains—particularly exclusive interior design firms and furniture stores.

The family has several of its own liquor brands and recently managed to negotiate an agreement with the State to subsidize cigarettes to soldiers at the fronts. This has brought Egato into conflict with the Umoriti family but Egato is a family with big plans and does not enter fights it doesn't think it can win.

### Kordar

Kordar is the family that has control over the banking system and currency. The members own stately buildings, wear beautiful clothes, drive luxury cars and can be said to form the foundation for all of clan Zovrin.

Kordar is, however, a family with problems, there is plenty of discord within it and the greed among members never wants to end, they betray their closest for the highest possible return. Arrogance and contempt have begun to result in violence and the other families within the clan look hungrily at how the eternal power struggle means that Kordar loses more and more power and initiative.

### Masteri

The Masteri family runs gaming halls and entertainment establishments, everything from stately casinos to betting firms, bars and theaters. Masteri focuses on delivering pleasures and playing on obsession and exploits people's weaknesses and dreams of a happy future.

Members of the Masteri family manipulate games and matches, bribe judges and athletes and also have contacts far down among the criminal gangs. They themselves are almost obsessed with games and gambling and often challenge each other.

### Morganti

The members of the cunning Morganti family have been merchants for centuries. They buy and sell everything—their transports go along rivers and railways and with their well-sharpened tongues they get the best prices and the rarest equipment.

After a newspaper scandal that accused the family of having redirected food that was on its way to soldiers at the eastern front so that it instead ended up in one of the family's work camps, they have become more cautious but behind the scenes they have begun to challenge clan Guiskard more and more, by among other things leading the mudslinging against it.

### Paninaro

The Paninaro family owns large amounts of real estate and controls construction firms and architectural offices. It is usually Paninaro who is responsible for the new constructions that take place in Sandukar.

Paninaro has come into conflict with the Straker family of clan Arkady, founder of the city's original architecture, and this conflict has led to state commissioners increasingly beginning to interfere in Paninaro's affairs. The family members are, however, known for their courage and do not hesitate to challenge state statutes.

### Umoriti

Humanity's greatest vice during the last hundred years is almost entirely under Umoriti's control since the family has almost complete power over the tobacco industry. Umoriti owns several tobacco plantations and factories, invests massive sums in marketing and launches brands aimed at personal style and taste.

Many of the family's employees receive part of their wages paid in cigarettes which they can sell on or use as barter goods. The Egato family, within their own clan, has come to challenge Umoriti's power and it is only a matter of when Umoriti will strike back.

*"Some stories are better off not being told."*
*— Mercurian Corso*

The day had started badly and looked set to end worse.
The only good thing the massive rain brought was keeping down the city's stench, rinsing away the refuse that its inhabitants mindlessly scattered about.
Mordekai turned around, raised his hand to his brow and squinted through the rain. The alley behind the bar was poorly lit and a single bare bulb struggled to bring hope against the rain's relentless assault.
He recognized the voice, knew immediately who it was standing behind him. The silhouette was illuminated when a car's headlights out by the street swept past and the contours revealed a slender, tall woman clad in a tight wet coat.
For a brief second he saw her within himself, how the sil-houette came to life, features and expression. The memories washed over him like a kick in the gut. He remembered how the two had spent days on end at Reerdon's hotel down by Västerparken, how they had loved, slept, drunk and loved, over and over again. How he had been calm during this brief time, how the light within him had penetrated the dark shimmer that surrounded his rigid heart. She had left him. Who could blame her for that, he was who he was. His heart was black as night.
But still the meeting, this meeting in a dark, cold and rainy alley, made him feel excitement and a certain anticipation. His dark heart pounded faster in his chest.
A raindrop ran down over his cheek and into the corner of his mouth where his smile took form.
"Stelia?"
He noticed that his voice trembled slightly. The silhouette took a couple of steps forward. The stiletto-heeled high boots broke the puddles and the woman's face was reached by the bulb's cold white light. Her skin was still pale as the finest imported porcelain, her dark green eyes appeared black in the gloom under the wet hat's brim.
The eyes he had lost himself in dreams of, which had given him a calm like a mother comforting a sad child.
These now dark and cold eyes.
"Mordekai."
The name was said low and her perfectly red-colored lips moved faintly. Her voice, the husky voice that had whispered endearments in his ear long ago.
She lifted her head slightly and brushed a wet blonde lock from her face with a gloved hand.
"I knew you were here. You're so predictable."
It sounded almost mocking, but Mordekai couldn't quite distinguish if it was meant that way through the rain's drum-ming against the overflowing garbage cans and tin roofs.
She seemed to be waiting. It made him suddenly feel uncertain.
"Stelia, what the hell do you want? We haven't seen each other in over a year."
And what a year of longing it had been.
"You disappeared when I was out getting cigar-ettes. Without a sound, gone, poof!"
He raised his voice to cut through the patter-ing rain. He slowly took a couple of steps forward.
"Mordekai, darling. Don't think it was your fault. I'm not right for you. I'm not right for anyone. You gave me an embrace to fall asleep in, a moment when I could forget myself and become someone else. You gave me memories I
still carry with me."
Her mouth was still hard and stern. Her eyes hidden by the gloom. Her voice sounded sorrowful. The rain pounded violent-ly down around the two people in the alley.
"But darling, you know as well as I do that this city brings out the worst in people. I hadn't planned for us to meet again. The memories would have sufficed."
Mordekai suddenly felt uneasy. It went head to head with his awakened desire for her, her body, her scent, her warmth. Slightly confused, he blinked the rain from his eyes.
"What are you talking about? Why are you here?"
He took a step backward, to increase the distance.
The red lips suddenly showed a faint smile.
"This isn't personal, I really hope you understand that. This is business and this is what I do.
Regardless of target."
From the rain-wet coat the silhouette drew a large chrome automatic pistol with a matte black suppressor and aimed it straight at him.
Mordekai stared. He saw in detail how a rain-drop hit the muzzle of the suppressor, and how it slowly ran off the oiled surface. His right hand instinctively drew back to reach the revolver at his back below the heavy coat. He already knew he would never make it in time.
The woman's hand tensed barely noticeably and the pistol jerked. The powerful heat from the powder cloud that pressed the lead bullet through the suppressor scorched him. He noted knife-sharp how the empty casing that was ejected rotated through the air and gleamed in the bulb's light.

He had been right. The day ended worse.
The bullet bored in just below his right nipple.
He gasped, reflexively took two steps backward. His vision became blurred. His right arm stopped functioning just when he had felt the revolver grip in his palm. The pain took over all other impressions as he slowly fell down onto the cold street. His breathing was heavy and a hissing sound came from his chest. Blood and water colored the asphalt beneath him. He lay on his back, perceived only the cold rain dancing over his face.
In the distance he heard footsteps of high heels. She came up to him. Her face, half hidden, clenched tight. The bulb flickered irregularly in the rain.
The pistol in her hand seemed too heavy for her.
A faint wisp of smoke rose from the suppressor. He had forgotten how beautiful she was. She raised the pistol toward his head.
"Mordekai, darling. I've always liked your face. So you can keep that."
She smiled an almost tender smile. Mordekai fought against the pain numbing him. He felt how the darkness within him worked to get him going.
Wake him. The woman lowered the pistol toward his chest.
It was beautiful in its binary deadliness. It decided; life or death. Neither arms nor legs obeyed him.
"Sleep now, darling, let me help you rest again."
Wake him. He blinked frantically, he felt how the black rage bubbled. His heart beat unnaturally fast and loud. The sky was dark and the rain grew in strength.
She hesitated a second, bit her lip. Wake him. Wake him! He suddenly managed to clench his fist. She closed her eyes, drew her head back and sighed quietly out into nowhere. The darkness pumped in his veins, the pain ran out of him. He opened wide his dark eyes. He was close!
She opened her eyes, lowered her gaze and met his.
He awakened. She knew. A brief bow and she squeezed the trigger. Three times. The rain drummed against roofs and street. The only thing that could be heard was the casings' metallic dance against the wet asphalt. Behind her the bulb finally gave up its struggle and went out with a hiss.
Stelia bent down and gave the motionless man a light kiss on his wet forehead and gently closed his blank eyes with her palm. She rose, picked up the empty casings, and left the alley, and the memories, behind her.

The law wears black uniform and hard baton and strikes merci-lessly and without quarter down like a sledgehammer on those who dare abuse the freedom the State provides.
**Justice**
The laws in the Empire are enacted by the bureaucrats at the Ministry of Justice in Sandukar. They are written to first and foremost serve the State's interests, secondly to give the police support to carry out their work with the greatest effective-ness and lastly to protect the citizens.
The regulations around the laws and their interpretations are mas-sive and require a great work effort to comprehend.
**The Police**
The police's duties are to protect the State, appre-hend the State's enemies and investigate and combat common criminal activity among the citizens.
Generally the public has a rather low confidence in the police force as corruption and scandals taint the organization, even though individual individuals within it act with integrity and compassion.
To maintain control over the police, the State has several different types of organizations with different duties.
The uniformed police force, commonly called the order police, is seen patrolling out on the streets.
The order police are often first on scene at alarms, cordon off crime scenes, handle barricades and clear up minor crimes.
As a counterpart to the order police there is the criminal police which consists of plainclothes criminal investigators, interrogation leaders and crime scene investigators. Many are trained in psychology and various criminal-techno-logical subjects. The criminal police get, besides inves-tigating more complex crimes, to engage in important surveil-lance assignments and work undercover inside the criminal organizations.
Subordinate to the criminal police is the tactical unit which is used when heavier firepower is needed, such as during break-ins at armed suspects' locations or for protection of the detectives when they need to venture into unsafe parts of the city. The tactical unit is also used during riots and for clearing out among the destitute and criminals. Part of the tactical unit acts as escort for dignitaries and during trans-port of dangerous persons and theft-attractive goods.
In case of crime suspicion where the State is the plaintiff, the
State Police, popularly called Stapo, is always brought in. Often
Stapo comes in even before the criminal police have had time to notify them, which fuels the rumor that Stapo has moles and
**The Justice System**

informants everywhere within the police force. Stapo's methods are very effective and the resources enviable. When it comes to curbing activity that threatens the State, no means are shunned to achieve success.
Stapo operates directly under the Ministry of Security and has the right to take over cases from the criminal police if the State's interests are considered threatened. This often creates a harsh atmosphere between the detectives and Stapo, as wounded egos and bul-lying methods create resentment.
Within the Ministry of Truth there is the Commissariat, a mysterious section shrouded in darkness. For those who search in the archives for information about the Commissariat, very little is to be found. The commissaries constitute a small, very power-ful police force believed to be directly subordinate to the State's elite. The commissaries have the right to take over any cases whatsoever from both detectives and Stapo, can requisition personnel without objections and may pronounce sentences for crimes on the spot.
The myths about the commissaries are many, ranging from them possessing strange powers to them being the State's own murder-ers. It is considered a truth that if a commissary takes a citizen to the Ministry of Truth for interrogation, that person will not return to society. Before a commissary no one is safe—neither family personalities, high police chiefs nor industrial moguls.
**Ranks within the police**
The different service ranks that exist within the police force are in ascending order:
**Within the order police**
• Police aspirant
• Police constable
• Patrol chief
• Police group chief
• Police captain
**Within the criminal police**
• Criminal assistant
• Criminal inspector
• Criminal chief inspector
• Police captain
• Police director
**Within Stapo**
• Stapo lieutenant
• Stapo captain
• Stapo major
• Stapo colonel
• Stapo marshal
**Criminal Technology**
In a former museum near Sandukar's core is the police force's central criminal laboratory, or Crimlab as it is called within the police. It houses several thousand researchers, technicians and forensic pathologists working in different depart-ments, on different cases and projects. Criminal police also come here for training as it is here the methods used to solve crimes and find clues and evidence are developed, with technical knowledge and profiling.
The new era's criminal technology is viewed with skepticism by the old guard as they prefer using proven methods such as harsh interrogations or one-on-one conversations with suspects—who not infrequently "happen to fall" during the conversations.
This difference in the view of police work not infrequently breeds frus-tration and friction between younger and older colleagues.
The detectives who embrace the new technology in their crime-fighting gladly resort to the methods and techniques that Crimlab's prominent criminal psychologists develop when it comes to motive-searching and profiling of suspects.
Sometimes, however, specialists from Crimlab or someone from Stapo are called in to help with the investigation.
Stapo has a large number of well-educated people who trust the modern technology.
Stapo is the police instance within which the use of modern criminal technology has gone the furthest. Often Stapo operates very effectively and thoroughly with regard to profiling, motive-searching and qualified surveillance work.
Great focus is placed on crime scene analysis and securing of traces and potential evidence for future comparisons.
On the well-guarded laboratory building's many base-ment levels, detailed personal information, registration of activities and technical evidence are stored in long rows of archive cabinets. They also have cells where they keep the most interest-ing criminals, like the mentally ill serial killers, the cold criminal geniuses and similar deviants. These indi-viduals are studied and subjected to various experiments in the hunt for the human psyche's darkest depths. It is gener-ally known that the Mengal family has great influence over
Crimlab and it is usually their researchers who handle the most bizarre and macabre cases.
The evidence crime investigators find in service is usually taken to Crimlab if it cannot be utilized directly at the local police station. It is routine at crime scenes to search for and document fingerprints and to photograph the actual crime scene from different angles so that one can see what it looked like even at later times. Clothing fibers, cartridge casings, strands of hair and similar items are collected to be examined under microscope, analyzed and registered. Through visual comparisons by sharp-eyed employees, technical evid-ence can help in binding a person to a location.
**Stapo's departments**
Stapo consists of several distinct departments that are responsible for protection of different parts of the State's operations and for crushing society-subverting tendencies.
Department 1 — Personal protection and protection of state property
Department 2 — Infiltration and control over internal subversive groups & persons
Department 3 — Infiltration and control over external subversive groups and persons*
Department 4 — Surveillance of the church and secret societies
Department 5 — Administration, auditing and logistics
Department 6 — Counterespionage
Department 7 — (secret)
Department 8 — Border control and identification
Department 9 — Special operations, assassinations and abductions
* This includes the enemy.

A skilled forensic pathologist can determine the time of death of a victim through a quick examination of the body. If the victim is examined within a day of death, accuracy is within an hour thanks to liver temperature. If the body is examined within a week, a probable date of death can be determined.

In cases where there are injuries to the body caused by, for example, knives, or if severe violence is evident, such as strangulation, the forensic pathologist can with high probability determine whether the killer is right- or left-handed. If the victim has been subjected to sexual violence, this can be established through examination of genitals and body orifices. A completed autopsy provides answers as to whether the cause of death was blood loss, oxygen deprivation, poisoning, brain damage, natural causes, etcetera.

Bullets can be matched to caliber and type of weapon through ballistics examination and rifling inspection under microscope. This requires bullets in good condition to compare with. Gunshot wounds can indicate the approximate distance between victim and shooter and from which angle the shot must have hit.

Court & Legal Process

When sufficient evidence has been gathered against a suspect, the criminal police contact the Justice Ministry's local prosecutor division to submit their report. The selected prosecutor takes on the case and prepares the trial. The suspect meanwhile sits in detention in the police district's central jail until the trial and is given the opportunity to speak with a defense attorney. In rare cases, the defense attorney and prosecutor can agree that the suspect should be released until the trial occurs, but then against a substantial bail sum. Worth noting is that truly powerful family personalities, State ministers and successful industrial leaders have legal immunity, which means they cannot be prosecuted, regardless of crime, as long as it does not concern High Treason.

A trial usually occurs within one to a couple of weeks, depending on the case and perpetrator. Simpler cases end up in the lower court where sentences are pronounced assembly-line style — it's rare that a case takes more than five minutes from description to verdict. In the higher court, more complicated crimes are taken up. Here the trial can take days or even weeks depending on the circumstances.

In the lower court there is a single judge who pronounces the verdict. In the higher court there are instead three judges, one of whom is a magistrate. Common to both courts is the black, hooded garment and that the judge's face is always covered by a mask that prevents recognition.

In the higher court there is always a professional jury with prominent people from different parts of society selected by the State — businesspeople, military officers, bureaucrats and doctors. The jury is only advisory and after a case is concluded but before the verdict falls, the jury gives the trio of judges their analysis and recommendation, which the judges have every right to ignore.

When the verdict falls, the accused is either acquitted or convicted. In an acquittal, the person is immediately released, without any claims on the legal apparatus — he/she should be grateful that justice prevailed, other compensation is superfluous. If the person is convicted, the punishment is announced in conjunction with the conviction.

It's not uncommon for trials where famous people figure, either as accused, witnesses or victims, to attract reporters in droves, which turns the entire legal process into a media circus with radio segments, reports and investigative journalism that gets column meter after column meter in the newspapers. Nothing attracts readers like juicy scandals in the chambers of high society.

### Punishment

When a person is convicted of a crime, regardless of severity, the person enters the state criminal register and receives a new citizen's passport where the conviction is clearly noted. This usually causes problems when purchasing restricted goods, financial transactions and employment options.

Common punishments are penal labor or prison, often in conjunction with fines that partially go to the State and partially to the crime victim. There are several prisons in the Empire, but the most notorious is Kasterov Fort on the island of Kasterov a couple miles outside Sandukar's harbors. Kasterov Fort houses a large number of hardened criminals and it's rare that a day passes there without some prisoner dying. Attempting to escape is more or less doomed, but that doesn't stop escape attempts from being carried out, either across the sea or through the special underground railway that runs between the island and Sandukar.

An alternative that men are often given when determining punishment is to be sent to the front during the sentence period and fight for the nation's honor and success with courage and blood. Many desperate souls see this as a better alternative than longingly staring out through a damp slot for the coming years. Women instead get the alternative to serve as bondservants during the sentence period, at one of the state institutions for companionship.

The death penalty occurs but is relatively rare — it's primarily for criminal activity against the State that it's pronounced. Common citizens are sentenced to execution by burning in one of the state incinerators. More prominent people can themselves choose whether they wish to be executed by bullet, rope, poison or electricity.

### Laws

The Empire's law books are thick and filled with clauses and bureaucratic incomprehensibilities. Many of the laws are clear, such as that one shall not kill, steal or assault, but in addition to them there is a multitude of ordinances that affect everyday life for the Empire's population.

That a law is enacted doesn't mean it's followed, but breaking it always entails a risk and if the police want to get someone, they take every opportunity that comes their way. Social status, class, ethnicity and gender play a role in criminal investigations. Crimes against disliked minorities in society often remain unsolved.

To speak negatively or seditiously toward the State in the form of speech, image, writing, music, theater or similar is punishable, often with prison, penal labor or death penalty as consequence. This law is overseen by the Ministry of Truth which monitors that no public information contains non-state propaganda. However, there are underground magazines and radio programs that broadcast from secret locations.

Not reporting slander or sedition toward the State is a serious crime. Persons who have made themselves guilty of such offenses shall immediately be reported by any witnesses.

Crimes between family members can be resolved through dueling, if both parties so wish. These so-called honor duels are always to the death and the winner is completely acquitted of any suspicions.

**Homosexuality and other sexual deviations from** the norm on an emotional level are seen as diseases that can be cured with various forms of therapy and experimental treatments. Prominent doctors from the Mengal family gladly demonstrate electricity therapy as a very good remedy.

To practice deviant physical intercourse, such as sodomy or oral sex, is criminal and can lead to fines or prison. This law is naturally very difficult to enforce as people usually engage in that type of activity privately and covertly.

A woman in a marriage is not considered able to be raped by her husband, as she cannot deny her husband intercourse. Possibly the man can in very rare cases be convicted of assault. Generally, rape is a crime that is seldom followed up on at all.

The law gives no extra protection to minors under twelve years, but children are often sentenced as if they were adults. In the same way, there are no special laws concerning intercourse with young individuals.

Abortions are illegal. This doesn't prevent them from occurring, however; many black clinics deal exclusively with abortions but prices are high if one wants to ensure not getting complications from the procedures. At higher levels in society, private doctors can usually take care of these procedures without asking any questions.

Prostitution is only legal at special state brothels. The majority of prostitutes in the Empire, however, don't belong to any of these brothels. This means they are vulnerable, both to violent customers and to the law's guardians who often exploit them for their own lusts' sake.

Children who are placed in orphanages and not adopted before they turn twelve can be confiscated by the State and placed in labor camps.

*"Crime is not for amateurs. There are fifty ways to screw it up. If you can think of half of them, you're a genius. And you're no genius."*
— Franko Millovy

Religion has left large marks in history but since the State was formed, the church has been humiliated and forced to its knees.

### The Church's Role

In Sandukar it's no longer considered normal to seek a higher power together with others. The church is opposed partly through bureaucracy, partly through eager enlightenment campaigns from the Ministry of Truth.

As a religious practitioner one is regarded as something of a desperate fanatic who doesn't quite fit into society. That the enemy in the east has taken religion to its heart and fights in the name of the One doesn't make things easier for the faithful, and many now choose to keep their faith secret from outsiders, to avoid persecution.

But still there exists a need for spiritual guidance and the brave are not scared by society's opinion but seek out the churches at services. Others content themselves with taking out the holy scripture and reading it when no one is watching to find comfort and security.

### History

The church's origin is shrouded in darkness; it seems to have existed in one way or another in all seven provinces, imprinted since ancient times. In ancient writings deep down in old church archives it can be found that the great western branch of religion, Janoism, has its origin in Madrostav. The teaching of the One god spread thanks to a preacher named Janos, who brought several traveling cults with him.

The cults crossed the country's borders but it was first when Madrostav's regent Karlof VII Alexos converted to the One god's teaching that Janoism truly spread.

The religion was well received in most surrounding kingdoms, especially by rulers, as the teaching advocated that the church should keep away from worldly power and instead concentrate on the spiritual. The people toiled to build churches that sanctified the faith and paid what they themselves felt they could spare to the church.

Around the same time another teaching began to spread in the eastern kingdoms, with its seat in Sadovia. It also built on an interpretation of the One god's commandments and was given the name Maurism. Maurism held that the church should be the ruling part in society as the priesthood were those who interpreted the One god's commandments. The church taxed the people

**The Church of the One**

and had black cathedrals erected where priests frightened the people into obedience with the horrors that awaited them if they violated the foundations of faith.

Those who openly questioned the Maurist priests were subjected to persecution and those who protested loudest disappeared.

### The Conflict

When the two doctrines met, the conflict became violent. What began in heated discussions soon led to war and conquests on both sides. The religious power struggle stretched from the smallest hovel to the greatest palace and dragged countries and peoples into massacres and destruction of sanctuaries. A
cold war then came to prevail as Maurism maintained its iron grip on the eastern parts while Janoism's doctrine was generally accepted in most of the remaining areas.

Darost had the priesthood banished as many of the inhabitants were secretly Maurists, and in Kovan an uprising occurred among the people, supported by the Zovrin clan, which led to the Maurist priests being slain and the cathedrals burned. After this, Kovan never let any religious doctrine in again.

The State & the Enemy
When the war against the enemy in the north descended like darkness over the realms, all countries were affected. The alliance between the seven clans had to have the church on its side in forming a strong state that could withstand and repel the enemy. This required Janoism and Maurism to make peace, and even though neither faith was happy about the idea, they supported it and the State's emergence in exchange for being allowed to continue spreading and proclaiming their doctrines.

Once the State had been formed and the united realms beaten back the enemy, there was hope for a brighter time.
Centuries of war and conflicts between the countries were over, the church was united and priests from both doctrines blessed the soldiers on the battlefields. Those who saw beyond the facade, however, noticed how the rulers acquired more and more power, and when the war was finally said to be over, they had the realms in an iron grip—as well as the church. They forced religion to give up its power and assets, Janoism became the fundamental doctrine and Maurism was more or less completely banned. Slowly the State began working to rule over the Empire alone.

### The Split

When it looked as though religion was completely dead and powerless and the State stood as the obvious victor, something happened that no one could have foreseen: the realm of
Presvedonia, which had always maintained a strong religious faith, got a new spiritual leader who called himself the Fallen
One. According to documents from that time, the new leader managed to seduce both the people of Presvedonia and the
Todescu clan into rebelling against the State. Inciting, powerful speeches and condemning threats caused the masses to rise and show what strength and hatred can be ignited by a strong faith.

The soldiers bore crosses and chanted the One God's commandments, the priesthood was among the trenches and the mighty cathedrals were overcrowded. A variant of Maurism had been created, and it took the name Siferism after the
Fallen One's heavenly name, Siferos. The faith proclaimed that the Hraghian people were chosen and that they should fight for their freedom after millennia of oppression.

Many of the openly faithful in the Empire lost their jobs, were subjected to extensive state interrogations and some were imprisoned or executed. The Ministry of Truth worked at high pressure to educate the people about the falsehoods in the spiritual poison that religion was.

But within the church they have realized that religion still has power, the State does not dare crush the church completely for fear that the fires of revolt will spread. Despite this, the church has gone out and condemned the Siferists and their actions even though many are secretly impressed by their strength and courage to fight for the One God, in a time when few dare confess their need for spiritual guidance.

The church is studying what is happening and even though its members are oppressed, it knows it has support among large parts of the people. More and more Maurists are beginning to appear in its ranks, they whisper in secret about retaking power and reforming the State into a religious one.
Others only want religious freedom but for now the State is too powerful and the church allows itself to be cowed while waiting for change and for an opportunity to achieve restoration.

### The Religious Branches

There are three branches within what is called the church:
Janoism, Maurism and Siferism.

### Janoism

The religious faction Janoism, originating in Madrostav, is based on interpretations by Janos, a simple man who after a tragedy that cost him his entire family restored religion by frenziedly reinterpreting and translating the ancient scriptures.
He proclaimed that the church and the people were one and that faith fundamentally lay with the individual—together, however, one could accomplish great deeds. The most important thing was not that one performed all the ceremonies, the most important thing was that one prayed and acknowledged the One God and his son, the savior, who sacrificed himself for humanity's sins.

### Playing the Faithful

There are many who pray to the
One God as in human nature there is a longing to understand and be part of something greater.
However, only a few are openly faithful, visit the churches and participate in masses. They are regarded with suspicion and must be prepared to be extra scrutinized when the State searches for traitors and insurrectionists. Fundamentally, however, there is not much difference between a believer and a non-believer.
The state laws resemble in many ways the heavenly ones so the greatest difference lies in the individual's view of the world and of their role in the grand scheme.
Playing a member of the priesthood requires choosing one's religious orientation and how much one is willing to risk to advance one's commandments. Is one willing to go as far as dying for one's faith? As part of the church one is included in a world of mysticism and forgotten knowledge. A believer is in many ways a seeker and the more one seeks and scrutinizes the world, the more cracks one can find in the State's facade. Even within the church, however, there are plenty of secret societies and orders that act for their own purposes. Some of them have, somewhat contradictorily, connections high up in the clans.

Janoism advocates hard work and abstinence and that one lives in harmony with nature, follows the ten commandments and guards against the seven sins. However, it is not a condemning doctrine but even the darkest of souls can find forgiveness. Likewise, Janos believed that church and state should be kept separate as the church should concentrate on the spiritual and the heavenly world while worldly governance should be left to the rulers. Janoism is now the only doctrine accepted by the State.

### Maurism

Maurism has its basis in the Maurean gospel, a gospel that is not recognized by the Janoists as it is said to have been written much later. The Maurean gospel is filled with mysticism, difficult-to-interpret prophecies and divine laws for how peoples shall be governed under the One God's commandments.

According to the gospel, the people shall submit to the One
God. Forgiveness and grace cannot be counted on if one breaks his ordinances, but rather a sojourn filled with eternal torment in the afterlife. On the other hand, one will receive wealth and power and a chance to rise in society if one serves him well. Those who have served the One
God devotedly and carried out his commandments can also become saints and be worshipped and depicted on icons and statues.

Maurism's followers have erected mighty cathedrals to demonstrate the One God's power. Stately statues of angels and demons show the heavenly and the infernal, and mighty painted glass windows illustrate events from the holy scriptures.

In addition to the holy scripture and the Maurean gospel, there are several saints' gospels where the saints' commandments are proclaimed. The use of rituals and ceremonies is great within Maurism, and many of the devout engage in various forms of flagellant rites to strengthen their faith.

Maurism is forbidden as a doctrine by the State and all documents mentioning the branch are copied and archived by the Archival Ministry. The orientation still exists relatively openly in the east and there are many within the church, even in Sandukar, who are secretly Maurists and hope for a religious order where the church rules.

### Siferism

Siferism is a variant of Maurism but has some different viewpoints, among other things the Hraghian people are considered chosen and urged to fight to free themselves from slavery. Siferism's leader, the myth-shrouded Siferos, is said to have been cast down from heaven and divinity to help humanity find god and simultaneously find their own atonement. By many he is regarded as the new Messiah.

In one of his most famous and well-documented speeches,
Siferos asserts that Sandukar is the place on earth where
Evil resides and that to achieve final purification
Sandukar must be burned to dust for 77 days. All copies and transcriptions of this speech, commonly called the
Liberation Speech, that are found by the State's troops are immediately burned and individuals found with the speech are immediately taken in for interrogation by the Commissariat.
The Liberation Speech is seen by many followers as the sign of Siferos's divinity and is therefore often carried close to the heart.

The church pews stand empty. Flickering wax candles force back the shadows from the altar passage but otherwise the building is immersed in darkness. Outside the sooty snow falls and frost has spread along the painted glass windows.
The church breathes peace and stillness but its halls are no longer visited, people are too afraid or have lost faith. Sebastian Okra looks out over the One's house and remembers the time when the pews were overcrowded, when the church was a symbol of warmth, peace and light. How has it gone so far?

The congregation's fall began with the expansion of
Werner & Quildo's factories. A new hope for the future it was called. A new bright time for the area where no one would need to go unemployed or hungry. It sounded fine on paper and in the slogans but the truth became quite different. Those who owned the factories only thought about profit, through bureaucratic trickery they dissolved the workers' contracts and offered them new ones and when the alternative stood between a worse agreement and unemployment, most were forced to accept.

The workers lost large parts of their wages, their rights and their security. The sooty fallout made them sick and many injured themselves in the machine presses and cisterns.

Sebastian noticed the difference in the congregation and heard during confessions how the people in his flock became increasingly desperate. The money was no longer enough, those who tried to organize against management were harassed or dismissed, the children were sick practically all the time and the work was harder than ever. Sebastian's flock was however still loyal to him. Every Sunday they came to service and many times they sought him out during the evenings to ask for advice. Sebastian himself visited many of the sick who could not come to church and when he saw the suffering they were in he himself made contact with the leader of Werner & Quildo.

The meeting did not go well, the aged Lester Quildo openly mocked both the workers, Sebastian and the church.
In an angry monologue he proclaimed himself an enemy of the church, he even threatened Sebastian and ordered him to leave the congregation. Sebastian refused and thereafter the sabotage and harassment began.

It started relatively quietly, the workers were advised not to visit the church henceforth. Worker meetings were scheduled at the same time as the services and when
Sebastian changed the church's times, the time for the worker meetings was changed shortly thereafter. The congregation shrank, but the most faithful remained. Each time they came they seemed more emaciated, the clothes hung like rags on their thin bodies. Their pale faces and pleading eyes were directed at Sebastian when he preached about how the truly faithful will get to experience true peace in the kingdom of heaven. He spoke of the One's goodness and began increasingly often to see the doubt in their gazes, but they so wanted to believe.

Sebastian chose to fight back, he began during his services to openly speak against the oppression that the workers felt. He supported Karl Erwald, a factory worker who took the matter in hand and began organizing a resistance against the factory management. He met members from his congregation in secret as they did not want to be harassed and services were held at home in his apartment. But for money everything can be bought, informants soon revealed what was going on and the punishment came in the form of anonymous threatening letters and assault of the congregation's members.

The electricity and heating were shut off in the church due to an "unknown technical error" and during the cold months the church transforms into an ice palace that was impossible to stay in for long periods. The church leadership's pressure was ineffective and finally it seemed as if Lester Quildo had won and the church lost.

Nowadays almost no one dares come here. Sebastian
Okra walks alone through the dark building and prays to the One for guidance and help. He has spoken with his superiors and in secret they have decided what must be done. A man within the church has been hired, a man with special abilities. This man shall ensure that Lester Quildo disappears. The church's piety only extends so far. A priest must look after his flock and if the flock is oppressed and harmed the guilty must be punished. Manufacturer Lester
Quildo will soon feel the One's wrath.

## Chapter 5 – Influence

It was one of those evenings, a hot, windless evening that breeds only three things; booze, lust and sweat. I snuffed out my third cigarette for the evening and looked out over the aimless flock of sheep all searching for a body to drunkenly crawl next to at closing time.
He stood alone at the inner bar. Abandoned. Awkward. He wasn't in con-trol at a place like this. I took one last look in the pocket mirror and saw with satisfaction that the blonde wig sat perfectly.
The man was about twice my age. Handsome with graying, thin hair. Faint age lines on his face. Slim and well-preserved body. Manicured and sensitive hands. Round glasses with metal frames. Sharp and expensive suit. Wedding ring in gold. A fat wallet. He was perfect. Meek, lonely and longing.
I walked toward him in my carefully chosen outfit for the evening. I positioned myself close to him, so he would detect my perfume. He turned his face toward me. I moistened my lips and pulled out a cigarette. While he fumbled for a lighter I leaned close to his ear.
"You seem like you need a friend."
He nodded, surprised. He was caught. I moved closer. In profile so the curve of my breasts was accentuated, crouched slightly so he could look down into the neckline. A discreet sigh came from him.
It was almost too easy.
He looked bewildered. Slightly sweaty on the forehead. I blew out the smoke, away from him, and turned back toward him. He cleared his throat and finally spoke, with a slightly cracked voice.
"So, I'm..."
He got no further before I pressed my gloved finger over his lips. I slowly shook my head.
"No names, friend. That's not what we're here for."
He seemed feverishly searching for something to say but before he got that far I locked his gaze.
Camouflaged behind an innocent smile I whispered sweetly.
"What do you say, friend, a classic you and me, somewhere else?"
His hazy gaze flared up and his breathing answered for him. I stroked him gently across the cheek and didn't look away.
He's hooked, and once again I'm going to be rich.
— Talia Navarre, Club Karisma, a Saturday in November

### Influence

Influence covers the fundamental rules and frame-work for playing Noir. Everything from action resolu-tion and how dice should be rolled to drama-creating mechanisms like fate and drives are covered.
Additionally, how a protagonist is constructed and the parts included in their profile are described in detail. Feel free to use the table of contents to quickly find the rules being sought.
**System**
The system in Noir helps narrator and players consistently and reasonably determine whether actions succeed. It exists primarily to create randomness which in turn creates drama.
In a novel, for example, an action's outcome is given by the author's desire, while action in role-playing is based on what participants wish to attempt to perform in the game world and in the current scene. The system provides conditions for desired actions to be executed in a successful way.
**Vision**
Noir's rules system is created according to a vision that builds on the system being:
• Simple
• Flexible & consistent
• Inspiring
• Reasonable
**Simple**
That the system should be simple means that few steps should be included in it. Both players and narrator should quickly understand how the system works and how it should be interpreted.
Flexible & consistent
By flexibility and consistency is meant that the same com-pact rules core should recur in all parts of the rules system.
There should be no exceptions or special solutions for different cases, but the system should be usable in all situations.
**Inspiring**
It may sound strange to say that a system should be inspiring — a system is fundamentally just rules, mathematics and probabilities. By inspiration is meant in this case that participants should be able to use the system based on their own characters to create dynamics

in the story. It's about allowing will over ability which means that built into the system are elements that make it possible to produce the utmost, the desperate power in hopeless situations and use it in dramatic ways. A competent but willless person performs a given task worse than a determined amateur. By incorporating personal gain, conviction and independent influence in the story's scenes, the engagement and immersion of the participants will increase, and make the entire experience stronger.
**Reasonable**
One of the fundamental pillars Noir rests on is that the experience and story take their departure in a reasonable world. To stop a train by grabbing hold of it is unreasonable, while stopping a cyclist in the same way is reasonable. However, it should be pointed out that Noir's world is not the same as our own and that protagonists can perform feats and/or heroic deeds that can be seen as unreasonable in our world, often through sacrifices or thanks to strong conviction. This is something that both narrator and players quickly learn — to set the level right for the experience they desire.
